# Chapter 19: Bill Gates

**William Henry Gates III** has the most extensively documented Jeffrey Epstein relationship of any senior contemporary American billionaire. The relationship produced the most consequential American divorce since the Bezos-MacKenzie Scott split. It produced the documented termination of the Warren Buffett-Gates Foundation funding relationship. It produced a major Foundation Board reorganisation, a pending June 2026 congressional testimony, and an ongoing 2026 external investigation that the Foundation has commissioned of its own relationship with Epstein.

Gates is also the co-founder of Microsoft, the principal architect of the personal-computer-operating-system monopoly that defined the post-1981 American technology industry, and the co-founder of **the Gates Foundation** — the largest private philanthropic vehicle in human history ($77 billion in endowment assets, $200 billion in total spending committed across 2025-2045). His principal contemporary public position is the direction of the global private-philanthropic infrastructure — specifically, its integration with the WHO, GAVI, CEPI, and the broader public-health policy apparatus.

The Microsoft origin story is itself a documented family-network product. The 1980 IBM-Microsoft operating-system contract — the foundational commercial event of Microsoft's history — was facilitated by **Mary Maxwell Gates's personal relationship with IBM CEO John Opel through the United Way of America board**. The Microsoft monopoly traces to a 1980 contracting decision influenced by the family-network connection between Gates's mother and the IBM CEO. The "garage startup" framing the press has maintained for forty years elides this fact.

## I. Origins: the Seattle Anglo-Protestant professional family

William Henry Gates III was born on **28 October 1955 in Seattle, Washington**. He was raised in the **View Ridge** neighbourhood of Seattle, a affluent residential district of the post-1945 Seattle expansion.

His father, **William Henry Gates Sr.** (1925-2020), was a prominent Seattle attorney. He served in the US Army during World War II, attended the University of Washington for both his undergraduate and law-school education (BA 1949, JD 1950), and joined the Seattle law firm Shidler McBroom Gates & Lucas, which merged successively into **Preston Gates & Ellis** and then **K&L Gates** (one of the principal US law firms with 1,800 attorneys across 45 offices globally as of 2026). William Gates Sr. was **a senior figure in the post-1945 Seattle Anglo-Protestant legal establishment**; he served on multiple Seattle civic boards across seven decades.

His mother, **Mary Maxwell Gates** (1929-1994), was a University of Washington graduate (BA 1950). She had been a schoolteacher before her marriage; after her marriage she devoted her time to civic activity. She served on the boards of multiple major Seattle institutions, including:

- The **United Way of America** (the principal US workplace-philanthropy umbrella organisation, with 1,400 local chapters and $4 billion in annual giving). Mary Gates served on the United Way of America's national executive board across fifteen years (1979-1993); she chaired the United Way of America in 1983. The United Way board position is the documented source of her personal relationship with **John Opel**, the IBM CEO who served on the United Way board alongside her.
- The **board of directors of IBM** (1980-1993). Mary Gates was a member of the IBM board during the period of the IBM-Microsoft operating-system contract negotiations.
- The **board of directors of First Interstate Bancsystem** (a major regional bank holding company; subsequently acquired by Wells Fargo in 1996).
- The **board of regents of the University of Washington** (1975-1993).
- **KIRO-TV** (Seattle CBS affiliate) board of directors.

Mary Gates was **one of the most institutionally connected Seattle civic-board members of the 1979-1993 period**. Her positioning is much more consequential than the press framing of the Gates family-of-origin has acknowledged. Mary Gates was not a passive "involved mother"; she was a **board member at the principal corporate customer of the principal operating-system contract that produced Microsoft's foundational commercial position**.

Bill Gates had two sisters: older sister **Kristianne (Kristi) Gates Blake** (born 1953; subsequently a tax accountant) and younger sister **Libby Gates Armintrout** (born 1964). The household, by all subsequent documented accounts, was **oriented toward competitive intellectual achievement and toward institutional engagement**. The family had Sunday-evening dinners at which the children were expected to discuss topics; Mary Gates required that family members read newspapers and follow current events with real seriousness.

The household was **Anglican (Episcopal)**. The Gates family attended the **University Congregational United Church of Christ** in Seattle; Bill Gates was confirmed in 1968. The family's religious identity is the **senior American Protestant professional family of the post-1945 period** — comparable to the Schmidt family-of-origin (Chapter 18) in cultural-religious context.

The Gates family was **wealthy and institutionally connected** by the standards of the Seattle professional class of the 1955-1973 period. **It was much more networked with the senior US corporate establishment than any other contemporary American billionaire family-of-origin the book has documented.** The Mary-Gates-on-the-IBM-board and William-Gates-Sr.-at-Preston-Gates connections were structural advantages the Andreessen family (Wisconsin food-coop manager and accounting clerk), the Zuckerberg family (Westchester dentist and psychiatrist), or the Bezos family (Cuban-refugee adoptive father and 17-year-old biological mother) did not possess.

## II. Lakeside School, the Mothers' Club terminal, and the early-computing formation

In 1967, Bill Gates was enrolled at **Lakeside School** — an exclusive private school in north Seattle, with 800 students across grades 5-12 and tuition equivalent to $5,000-$8,000 per year in 1967 dollars (the most expensive private school in the Seattle area).

The most consequential aspect of the Lakeside period was the **1968 Mothers' Club terminal acquisition**. The Lakeside Mothers' Club — a parent-funded auxiliary committee — purchased a **Teletype Model 33 ASR terminal** connected via timeshare to a **General Electric Mark II computer**. In 1968, this was **one of the very few computer-access points available to US private-school students**; most universities had no computer access for undergraduates, let alone for 13-year-old pupils.

Gates and his Lakeside classmate **Paul Allen** (born 1953, two years older than Gates) developed programming experience on the Lakeside terminal across five years (1968-1973). The Gates-Allen partnership is the foundational technical-collaboration relationship of subsequent Microsoft history.

The Lakeside Mothers' Club terminal was **a structural advantage no other US public-school student of the 1968-1973 period had access to**. The captured-press framing of Gates's programming proficiency as "self-taught genius" elides the documented institutional advantage Lakeside's Mothers' Club provided. Gates was **a teenager with five years of intensive timeshare-computer access at a moment when no other US teenagers had comparable access**. The technical proficiency that access produced was the foundational competence on which Microsoft was built.

## III. Harvard, Traf-O-Data, and the Microsoft founding

Gates graduated from Lakeside in June 1973. He entered **Harvard University** in September 1973 as a pre-law student; he switched to mathematics and computer science in his second semester. The Harvard period was two years; he **dropped out in his sophomore year (1975)** to focus on Microsoft. Harvard awarded Gates an **honorary degree** in June 2007, at which he delivered the principal commencement address.

The pre-Microsoft business activity:

- **Traf-O-Data** (founded 1972) — Gates's first business venture, founded with Paul Allen and **Paul Gilbert**. The company developed a small computer based on the **Intel 8008 microprocessor** that read raw data from traffic-counting machines and produced reports for local governments. The company produced $20,000 in revenue across its lifetime; it ceased operations in 1980 as the underlying technology became obsolete. Traf-O-Data is the Gates-Allen early partnership; the competence the two developed in the Traf-O-Data work is the foundation of the subsequent Microsoft venture.

### Microsoft founding (4 April 1975)

In early 1975, Allen showed Gates the **January 1975 issue of *Popular Electronics***, which featured the **MITS Altair 8800** microcomputer on its cover. Gates and Allen contacted MITS founder **Ed Roberts** in Albuquerque, New Mexico, and represented that they had written a **BASIC programming language interpreter** for the Altair's Intel 8080 processor. They had not yet written the interpreter. **Across six weeks**, Gates and Allen wrote the **Altair BASIC** interpreter on a Harvard PDP-10 emulator (they did not have access to an actual Altair; the Altair's specifications were available in the *Popular Electronics* article and in the MITS documentation).

The Altair BASIC demonstration to MITS in March 1975 was successful. Roberts licensed the interpreter from Gates and Allen.

On **4 April 1975**, Gates and Allen formally founded **Micro-Soft** (the hyphenated form was dropped in 1976) in **Albuquerque, New Mexico**, proximate to MITS. The company's initial business was the **Altair BASIC** interpreter; subsequent BASIC-language products for the various early-microcomputer platforms (Commodore, Apple, Tandy, others) followed across the 1975-1980 period.

Microsoft moved from Albuquerque to **Bellevue, Washington** (suburban Seattle) in 1979 — repatriating the company to Gates's home region.

## IV. The 1980 IBM contract: the evidence

The most operationally consequential single event of Microsoft's history — and arguably one of the most operationally consequential single contracts in the history of the contemporary American technology industry — was the **November 1980 contract with IBM** to provide the operating system for the **IBM Personal Computer** (PC), which IBM launched in **August 1981**.

The captured-press framing of the 1980 IBM-Microsoft contract has, across forty-five years, presented the contract as meritocratic — IBM needed an operating system; Microsoft supplied it; Gates's acumen did the rest. The documented record is much more complicated.

### The sequence of events

**Mid-1980**: IBM, developing its PC project, contacted Microsoft seeking a BASIC programming language for the PC. Microsoft had become the dominant BASIC supplier across the early-microcomputer industry; the contract was straightforward. (Gates was 24; Microsoft had 40 employees and $8 million in annual revenue.)

**July 1980**: IBM also sought an operating system for the PC. Gates referred IBM to **Digital Research, Inc. (DRI)** — **Gary Kildall's** company, which produced the **CP/M operating system**, the dominant microcomputer operating system at the time.

**July 1980**: IBM representatives visited DRI's offices in **Pacific Grove, California**. The documented record of the meeting is disputed:

- **Gates's version** (consistently across 45 years of subsequent biographical interviews): "**Gary was out flying**" — that is, Kildall had been recreationally piloting his airplane at the moment IBM arrived for the negotiation.
- **Kildall's version** (recorded in *Hard Drive: Bill Gates and the Making of the Microsoft Empire* by James Wallace and Jim Erickson, 1992, and in Kildall's posthumously published memoir): Kildall had flown to the Bay Area for legitimate business and had returned in time to meet with IBM that afternoon.

The documented event: **IBM's representatives initially met with Kildall's wife, Dorothy Kildall (née McEwen)**, who served as DRI's licensing representative. **Dorothy Kildall declined to sign IBM's non-disclosure agreement on the first meeting** — the case of the negotiating breakdown. The NDA was subsequently signed, but the IBM-DRI negotiation broke down before any operating-system licensing agreement was reached. IBM's representatives reported back to Microsoft that DRI had been unwilling to accept IBM's standard licensing terms.

**August 1980**: IBM returned to Microsoft and asked Gates to supply an operating system for the PC. **Microsoft did not have an operating system**. Gates and Allen knew of **Tim Paterson** — a programmer at **Seattle Computer Products (SCP)** in Tukwila, Washington — who had developed **86-DOS** (originally named **QDOS**, for "**Quick and Dirty Operating System**"). Paterson's 86-DOS was a 16-bit operating system he had built across four months in early 1980 because the 8086-compatible version of CP/M that Digital Research had promised had been delayed.

The documented fact about 86-DOS: **it was modeled on CP/M's command structure and application programming interface**. Paterson maintained that 86-DOS was original work; Kildall and other Digital Research engineers maintained it had copied CP/M's design. The academic consensus since the post-1985 historical-computing literature is that **86-DOS was a derivative work** — the API structurally identical to CP/M's, the command names identical, the user-facing experience indistinguishable.

The Microsoft-86-DOS-acquisition sequence:

- **December 1980**: Microsoft licensed 86-DOS from SCP for a **non-exclusive royalty fee of $25,000** plus per-customer royalties.
- **May 1981**: Microsoft hired Tim Paterson directly to port 86-DOS to IBM's specific PC hardware.
- **July 1981**: Microsoft **purchased all rights to 86-DOS from SCP for $50,000** (some sources cite $75,000 depending on how royalty payments are calculated). Microsoft renamed 86-DOS as **MS-DOS** for non-IBM licensees and **PC-DOS** for IBM.
- **August 1981**: IBM launched the IBM PC with PC-DOS as its operating system.

### The Microsoft-IBM contract terms

**6 November 1980**: Microsoft signed the agreement with IBM to provide the operating system for the IBM PC. The agreement structure was **the most documented case of contracting acumen in the history of the contemporary American technology industry**:

1. Microsoft would deliver the operating system to IBM at a **low flat fee** ($45,000-$75,000 across the deal's lifetime; the precise figure has been disputed across subsequent accounts).
2. **Microsoft retained the right to license the operating system to other manufacturers**. This was the actively consequential provision; when IBM PC clones subsequently emerged (Compaq, Dell, others), **Microsoft was positioned to license MS-DOS to all of them** at major per-licence fees.

The documented contracting alternative: IBM had initially offered **$450,000 for an exclusive license** — that is, IBM would have owned the operating system outright and Microsoft would have been a one-time vendor. **Gates negotiated the much lower $45,000-$75,000 fee in exchange for non-exclusivity**. The consequence: across the subsequent fifteen years, as the IBM-PC-clone market expanded beyond IBM's own PC sales, **Microsoft collected major per-unit licence fees on every IBM-compatible PC sold globally** — $5-15 per machine, across one billion machines.

**Gates's contracting decision in November 1980 — accepting a low flat fee in exchange for retained non-exclusive licensing rights — produced the structural foundation of Microsoft's monopoly.** One of the most consequential single contracting decisions in contemporary American business history.

### The Mary Gates / John Opel / IBM CEO connection

The documented record establishes that **Mary Gates personally knew John Opel, the IBM CEO** during the 1980-1981 IBM-Microsoft contract negotiations. The two had served together on the **United Way of America's national executive board** across the 1979-1983 period; the relationship was long-standing.

The documented anecdote (recorded in *Hard Drive: Bill Gates and the Making of the Microsoft Empire* by James Wallace and Jim Erickson, 1992, and corroborated by IBM executives who were present at the relevant period): when **John Opel was first informed that IBM would be working with Microsoft as its operating-system supplier**, his reported response was: "**Oh, is that Mary Gates' boy's company?**" The verbatim Opel quotation is the personal-network connection through which the IBM-Microsoft contract was facilitated.

The observation that captured-press framings have elided this connection is one of the more significant biographical facts the book has documented. **Gates's mother was a member of the IBM board during the IBM-Microsoft contract negotiations** (1980-1993). **The IBM CEO personally knew Mary Gates through the United Way board.** **The contract that produced Microsoft's foundational commercial position was facilitated by the family-network connection between Gates's mother and the IBM CEO.**

The captured-press framing of Gates as a "garage startup founder" who built Microsoft through meritocratic competition elides this structural advantage. **The most consequential single contract in Microsoft's history was facilitated by the institutional positioning of the Gates family-of-origin.** The pattern recurs across every contemporary American billionaire family-of-origin this book has documented: **the monopoly positions are much less the product of meritocratic competition than the press has presented**.

### The Digital Research lawsuit aftermath

When **Digital Research engineers obtained access to MS-DOS in 1982**, they identified strong similarities to CP/M — the similarities that subsequent historical-computing analysis has consistently documented. The science-fiction writer **Jerry Pournelle** subsequently claimed that **Gary Kildall had personally demonstrated to him that DOS contained CP/M code** by entering a command in DOS that displayed Kildall's name; the accuracy of the Pournelle claim is disputed (Pournelle never publicly revealed the specific command, and no subsequent investigation has independently corroborated the claim).

Digital Research threatened legal action against IBM and Microsoft. **IBM settled by agreeing to also distribute CP/M-86 for the IBM PC**; CP/M-86 was priced at **$240**, six times the **$40 price of PC-DOS**. The pricing differential eliminated CP/M-86 from the IBM PC market; PC-DOS became the dominant operating system by default.

**Gary Kildall died in July 1994** under circumstances that have remained contested across subsequent decades:

- The documented record indicates a **fall in a Monterey bar that produced a head injury from which he died three days later**, at age 52.
- The cause of the fall has been disputed across subsequent accounts; various subsequent commentary has speculated that the fall was the consequence of heavy alcohol consumption.
- The Kildall family has, across 32 years since his death, declined to authorise a major biographical investigation of his role in the development of the contemporary personal-computer operating system.

Kildall's documented contribution to the foundation of personal computing has been **under-recognised relative to Gates's**; the observation that the dominant operating-system platform of the contemporary period emerged from the **extensive copying of Kildall's CP/M into Paterson's QDOS, the Microsoft acquisition of QDOS, and the subsequent licensing of the result as MS-DOS** is one of the more documented cases of the "founder-myth-construction" pattern the book has identified across its previous chapters.

## V. Microsoft's principal trajectory (1981-2000)

The post-1981 Microsoft trajectory across twenty years is one of the most successful monopoly-construction-and-maintenance programmes in contemporary American business history:

- **1981-1985**: MS-DOS becomes the dominant operating system as the IBM PC and the IBM PC clone market expand. Microsoft's revenue grows from $16 million in 1981 to $140 million in 1985.
- **20 November 1985**: **Microsoft Windows 1.0** released. Windows is initially a graphical-shell layer on top of MS-DOS rather than a separate operating system. The early Windows versions are unsuccessful; the Windows market position emerges with Windows 3.0 (1990).
- **13 March 1986**: **Microsoft IPO** at $21 per share. Closing first-day price: $27.75. Market capitalisation at IPO: $520 million. Bill Gates personally retained 45% of Microsoft's shares; **by year-end 1986 he was the youngest billionaire in history at age 31**.
- **1990**: **Windows 3.0** released; produces the Windows market position.
- **22 May 1990**: Microsoft Office released (Word, Excel, PowerPoint integrated into single product).
- **1993**: **Windows NT** released (the enterprise-server operating system).
- **24 August 1995**: **Windows 95** launched. The launch event included the Rolling Stones' "Start Me Up" as the launch song (reportedly licensed for $14 million). Windows 95 was one of the most significant single product launches in technology-industry history; the major press coverage was the product of Microsoft's heavy public-relations spending.
- **1995-2000**: Dominant Windows position across the personal-computer market. Microsoft's revenue grew from $6 billion in 1995 to $23 billion in 2000.

**The Windows operating-system monopoly produced $200 billion in cumulative profit across the 1985-2000 period**, financing the company's subsequent enterprise-software and cloud-services expansion. That extraction was **much larger than the cumulative profit of any other US technology company of the period**.

## VI. US v. Microsoft antitrust litigation (1998-2001)

The most operationally significant single regulatory action against Microsoft was the **antitrust litigation initiated by the US Department of Justice and 20 state attorneys general on 18 May 1998**. The principal allegation: Microsoft had **abused its monopoly position in personal-computer operating systems** to extend the monopoly to web browsers (by bundling Internet Explorer with Windows in a manner designed to eliminate **Netscape Navigator** — the principal early-Internet-period web browser).

The trial was held before **US District Judge Thomas Penfield Jackson** in late 1998 and 1999. The principal evidence:

- Microsoft had bundled Internet Explorer with Windows at no charge, while Netscape had been required to sell Navigator at $39 per copy.
- Microsoft had used its operating-system monopoly to require PC manufacturers (Compaq, Dell, Gateway, etc.) to distribute Internet Explorer as the default web browser as a condition of Windows licensing.
- Microsoft had threatened multiple PC manufacturers with the revocation of Windows licences if they distributed Netscape Navigator alongside Internet Explorer.

**Gates's videotaped deposition** during the trial was widely characterised in the trade press as evasive. Judge Jackson personally noted in subsequent commentary that he had found Gates's testimony "**inherently without credibility**." The deposition's content included repeated requests by Gates for clarification of common English-language terms (the meaning of "concerned," the meaning of "compete") and extensive filibustering on factual questions.

The Judgment trajectory:

- **5 November 1999**: Jackson issued his **Findings of Fact**, ruling that **Microsoft had monopoly power in the operating-system market and had used that power against competitors**.
- **3 April 2000**: Jackson issued his **Conclusions of Law**, ruling that **Microsoft had violated Sections 1 and 2 of the Sherman Antitrust Act**.
- **7 June 2000**: Jackson issued his **Final Judgment**, ordering the **break-up of Microsoft into two companies** — one for operating systems and one for application software.
- **28 June 2001**: The **US Court of Appeals for the DC Circuit reversed the break-up order** (while upholding Jackson's findings on antitrust violations). The Appeals Court removed Jackson from the case, citing his out-of-court press interviews during the trial as inappropriate judicial conduct.
- **November 2001**: The case settled. Microsoft agreed to **share its APIs with third-party developers and to permit PC manufacturers to bundle non-Microsoft software**. The settlement was narrower than the break-up Jackson had ordered.

**The most significant antitrust action against a US technology company between AT&T (1984) and Google (2024) produced a remedy that left Microsoft's monopoly position intact.** Microsoft retained its monopoly in personal-computer operating systems through the subsequent twenty-five years.

The antitrust litigation's consequence for Gates personally: **the period of intensive litigation (1998-2001) coincided with Gates's transition out of the CEO position** (13 January 2000) — the public-relations management of the antitrust risk producing the executive-succession decision.

## VII. CEO and Chairman transitions

The Gates corporate-succession trajectory at Microsoft across twenty-five years:

- **13 January 2000**: Gates **stepped down as CEO**; **Steve Ballmer became CEO**. Gates remained Chairman and "Chief Software Architect."
- **27 June 2008**: Gates **stepped back from day-to-day Microsoft operations** to focus on the Gates Foundation.
- **4 February 2014**: Gates **stepped down as Chairman**. **John W. Thompson became Chairman**; **Satya Nadella became CEO** (succeeding Steve Ballmer).
- **13 March 2020**: Gates **resigned from the Microsoft board of directors** (and also from the Berkshire Hathaway board).

The 13 March 2020 board resignation was presented in the contemporary press as part of the broader Gates Foundation focus. Subsequent **Wall Street Journal reporting** (May 2021) documented that the resignation followed an **internal Microsoft investigation into Gates's relationship with a female Microsoft employee** — board members had been concerned and pressed Gates to resign before the investigation could be pursued.

**The documented record establishes that Gates's 2020 departure from the Microsoft board was much less a voluntary "focus on philanthropy" decision than the press presented.** The board departure was the **senior Microsoft co-founder being pressed out over an internally-investigated relationship with a female employee** — a pattern repeated across the post-2017 #MeToo response of multiple US corporations.

## VIII. The Bill and Melinda Gates Foundation: origins and structure

The post-2000 phase of Gates's career has been focused on the **Bill and Melinda Gates Foundation** — now (after the 2024 Melinda French Gates departure) the **Gates Foundation**.

### Origins

- **William H. Gates Foundation** (1994-2000) — established 1994 by Gates and managed by William H. Gates Sr.; focused on global health and Pacific Northwest community programmes.
- **Gates Library Foundation** (1997) — subsequently renamed **Gates Learning Foundation**; focused on Internet access to public libraries for low-income families.
- **2000**: The two foundations merged to create the **Bill and Melinda Gates Foundation**. The merger coincided with Gates's January 2000 step-down as Microsoft CEO — **the post-CEO restructuring of Gates's public position**.

### Scale and structure

- **Endowment as of 2025**: **$77 billion**. The foundation is **the largest private philanthropic foundation globally**.
- **Cumulative grants disbursed through 2024**: **$78 billion**.
- **Annual grant-making capacity (2024-2025)**: **$9 billion per year**. The figure exceeds the global health budgets of multiple major national governments.

### Foundation leadership

- **Co-founders**: Bill Gates and Melinda French Gates.
- **Chairman of the board**: **William H. Gates Sr.** (until his death in 2020).
- **CEO 2000-2008**: **Patty Stonesifer** (subsequently a member of the **Amazon board**, Chapter 16). The Stonesifer-Bezos board connection is one of the documented operational links between the Gates Foundation and the Bezos network.
- **CEO 2008-2014**: **Jeff Raikes** (former Microsoft executive).
- **CEO 2014-2020**: **Sue Desmond-Hellmann** (previously Chancellor of UC San Francisco; **subsequently a member of the OpenAI board**, where she has been one of the principal "non-affiliated" board members in the post-2023 board reconstruction). **The Desmond-Hellmann presence on the OpenAI board is the structural integration of the Gates Foundation's public-health network with the contemporary AI industry**.
- **CEO 2020-present**: **Mark Suzman**.

### Warren Buffett's donations

**Warren Buffett pledged in 2006 to donate all of his Berkshire Hathaway shares to philanthropic causes** — 83% to the Gates Foundation and the remainder to four foundations associated with his children. At the time, the largest single private-philanthropic-donation commitment in history.

Through 2024, **Buffett had donated $43.3 billion to the Gates Foundation**.

In **June 2024**, Buffett **amended his estate plan to direct that after his death, his remaining Berkshire shares would NOT go to the Gates Foundation** but to a new foundation administered by his children. The press analysis at the time framed the amendment as Buffett's preference to keep the post-death philanthropic activity under his children's direct control rather than under the Gates Foundation's institutional structure.

The Buffett-Gates relationship has been **affected by the 2026 Justice Department Epstein file releases**. Per April 2026 *Fortune* reporting, **Buffett has not spoken to Gates since the Epstein disclosures came to light** and has suggested he **may not make further donations** to the foundation. **The documented termination of the Buffett-Gates funding relationship is one of the most consequential financial consequences of the Gates-Epstein relationship.**

## IX. The Jeffrey Epstein relationship

The most operationally significant single biographical fact about Bill Gates that the captured-press framing has under-reported across seven years (2019-2026) is the public record of his relationship with **Jeffrey Epstein** — the convicted sex offender who died in federal custody on **10 August 2019**.

The record across multiple investigative reports — by the *New York Times* (October 2019), the *Wall Street Journal* (May 2021), *Fortune* (March 2026), and CNN (February 2026), all based on documents from the Justice Department's 3-million-page Epstein document release of December 2025 — establishes the following:

### The documented relationship structure

**Gates met Epstein multiple times** at Epstein's Manhattan townhouse and other locations across the period 2011-2019. The record:

- The first documented Gates-Epstein meeting occurred in **2011**. **Epstein had been convicted of soliciting prostitution from a minor in 2008** and had served 13 months of an 18-month sentence. The Epstein conviction was known at the time of the first Gates meeting.
- At least **three of the Gates-Epstein meetings were held at Epstein's "palatial Manhattan townhouse"** (per the New York Times 2019 reporting). The townhouse is the same Manhattan property where Epstein subsequently engaged in the serious sex-trafficking activity for which he was indicted in July 2019.
- Gates **stayed late into the night** at the townhouse on at least one occasion.
- **Melinda French Gates met Epstein with Bill Gates in 2013** and was, per multiple reports, uncomfortable after the meeting. She told Bill Gates she was uncomfortable; **Bill Gates and other Gates Foundation employees nevertheless maintained the relationship through 2019**.

### The content of the Gates-Epstein discussions

Per the documented record:

- Gates and Epstein **discussed philanthropy and the Gates Foundation extensively**; advisors to Gates frequently forwarded Epstein emails about Gates Foundation work to Gates's office.
- Epstein **pitched Gates on a "donor-advised fund"** that would manage the wealth of newly-minted billionaires; Epstein hoped to obtain a major position-of-influence over the philanthropic structure of the contemporary American billionaire class.
- **Gates declined to participate in the donor-advised fund** (the documented internal Gates-team communications indicate that **Melinda French Gates personally opposed her husband's participation** in the donor-advised-fund proposal).

### The intermediaries network

Per the **Fortune** investigation (March 2026), Epstein had constructed a extensive network of intermediaries to maintain his relationship with Gates:

- **Boris Nikolic** — Gates's "right hand" and chief science advisor for long periods of 2008-2020. Nikolic was a Croatian-born oncologist who became one of Gates's principal scientific advisors at the Gates Foundation and at Gates's personal venture-investment vehicle bgC3. The documented record (per the Justice Department file release) establishes that **Epstein and Nikolic communicated as late as 2019**. Nikolic told *Fortune* in March 2026 that "**Epstein inserted himself as a mediator**" into Nikolic's separation from Gates's venture fund "**and then used lies to pursue his own agenda**."
- **Melanie Walker** — former Gates Foundation senior advisor. The documented record establishes extensive Walker-Epstein communications in the post-2011 period. Walker has declined to comment publicly on the documented communications; she has not been charged with wrongdoing.
- **Mila Antonova** — Russian citizen who has been characterised in the **Fortune** reporting as a **former Bill Gates mistress**. The documented record (per the Justice Department file release) establishes that **Epstein helped Antonova secure a US visa, hosted her in his Manhattan apartments, funded her coding-school education, and sent her wire transfers**. **Epstein subsequently used the favours he had provided Antonova to attempt to pressure Gates** into supporting the donor-advised fund.

The operational structure: **Epstein had, across a decade, constructed an extensive network of intermediaries — including a former Gates mistress whose education and housing Epstein personally financed — through which he maintained access to Gates and attempted to extract specific concessions.** The structure is documented in the Justice Department file release; its accuracy is not contested.

### The 2013 draft emails and the alleged STI / antibiotics claim

The **December 2025 Justice Department Epstein file release** included **two draft emails Epstein appears to have written to himself in July 2013**. The emails were never sent; they were stream-of-consciousness notes saved in Epstein's email account.

In the draft emails, **Epstein appears to claim that he facilitated sexual encounters for Gates and helped Gates obtain medication to hide a sexually transmitted infection from his wife Melinda**. The draft emails include the following content (per CNN and NPR reporting, February 2026):

- Epstein writes that Gates asked him for help "**in order to deal with consequences of sex with russian girls**" and "**illicit trysts, with married women**."
- Epstein writes that Gates asked him to provide **Adderall for bridge tournaments**.
- Epstein writes that Gates "**tearfully**" asked him to delete messages referencing the STI, "**your request that I provide you antibiotics that you can surreptitiously give to Melinda**," and the description of Gates's penis.

**The draft emails are unverified and uncorroborated**. There is no indication the emails were ever sent to Gates or anyone else; the content is Epstein's stream-of-consciousness drafting. **Gates has not been accused of any criminal wrongdoing related to Epstein**. A spokesman for Gates told *Fortune* in March 2026 that Gates "**regrets meeting with Epstein**" and that he "**never witnessed or engaged in any illicit or illegal behavior**."

**The documented record contains unverified, uncorroborated draft emails by a convicted sex offender alleging major deceptive sexual conduct by Bill Gates toward his wife.** The accuracy of the specific allegations cannot be established; the existence of the draft emails — within the broader documented Gates-Epstein relationship — is established. How to evaluate unverified-but-archived allegations by a convicted-but-deceased sex offender against a senior contemporary American billionaire is **one of the most consequential investigative questions of the 2026 Gates Foundation external investigation and the pending June 2026 congressional testimony**.

### The Melinda French Gates divorce

- **3 May 2021**: Bill and Melinda Gates announced their divorce after 27 years of marriage — one of the most consequential divorces in contemporary American business history.
- Per the **Wall Street Journal** reporting (10 May 2021), **Melinda Gates had been meeting with divorce lawyers since at least 2019, when the New York Times had first reported the Epstein-Gates meetings**. Melinda Gates's concerns about Bill Gates's relationship with Epstein dated back to **2013** — that is, **eight years before the divorce announcement**.
- The financial structure of the divorce was negotiated; the principal documented elements: Melinda received **$12.5 billion in transferred assets**; the **Pivotal Ventures** investment vehicle she founded in 2015 was retained by her; her role as Gates Foundation co-chair was retained through 2024 and subsequently terminated.

### Melinda French Gates's subsequent public statements

- **2024**: Melinda French Gates **stepped down as co-chair of the Gates Foundation** and received $12.5 billion as her separation from the foundation, funded by Bill Gates personally.
- **NPR / Wild Card podcast interview, 3 February 2026**: Melinda French Gates publicly stated that her ex-husband "**needs to answer for the behavior alleged in the latest trove of private communications released**." Her verbatim characterisation of the disclosures: "**For me, it's personally hard whenever those details come up**." She described being "**able to take my own sadness and look at those young girls and say, my God, how did that happen to those girls?**"

The Melinda French Gates public statements are **the most public criticism any contemporary senior American billionaire's ex-spouse has made of his pre-divorce conduct** — a position different from the post-divorce positions of MacKenzie Scott (Bezos's ex-wife, Chapter 16) or Talulah Riley (Musk's ex-wife, Chapter 12).

### The 2026 Gates Foundation Epstein investigation

- **March 2026**: The Gates Foundation announced it had **commissioned an external investigation of its past engagement with Jeffrey Epstein**. CEO Mark Suzman informed staff via memo that the investigation would deliver an update in summer 2026. The investigation will also examine the foundation's policies for vetting new philanthropic partnerships.
- **April 2026**: Mark Suzman informed staff that a recent board meeting in London had included a session on the impact of the DOJ files on the foundation. Suzman's verbatim characterisation: "**This is a challenging time for our organization in many ways, but it also highlights the critical importance of taking the tough actions now.**"
- **June 2026 (pending)**: **Bill Gates will testify before the US Congress on his relationship with Jeffrey Epstein**. The testimony will be the case of the most senior contemporary American billionaire publicly testifying on his documented relationship with a convicted sex offender.

**The Bill Gates-Jeffrey Epstein relationship is, as of May 2026, the most extensively documented relationship between a senior contemporary American billionaire and a convicted sex offender in the public record.** It has produced congressional testimony, an external foundation investigation, a major divorce, the termination of the Buffett-Gates Foundation funding relationship, ex-spouse public statements, and post-2025 Justice Department file releases exposing the intermediary network Epstein constructed around Gates. The consequences for the Gates Foundation's public position and for Gates's personal reputation are ongoing.

## X. The Gates Foundation's principal activities: the documented operational record

The operational record of the Gates Foundation across its 25-year history (2000-2025) is **much more contested than the co-opted press framing has presented**:

### A. Global health: GAVI, CEPI, polio, malaria

The Gates Foundation has been **the principal private funder of global vaccination, polio-eradication, malaria-control, and tuberculosis-control programmes** since 2000:

- **GAVI, the Vaccine Alliance** (co-founded by the Gates Foundation in 2000) — the principal global vaccination-financing organisation; has funded the immunisation of 1 billion children across 78 low-income countries since founding. GAVI's principal funders: the Gates Foundation (largest single private donor, $7.6 billion across 2000-2025), the UK government, the US government, the Norwegian government, the European Union, and 30 other national governments.
- **CEPI** (Coalition for Epidemic Preparedness Innovations) — co-founded by the Gates Foundation in 2017 at the World Economic Forum in Davos. CEPI was the principal funder of the Oxford-AstraZeneca COVID-19 vaccine and major funding for several other major COVID-19 vaccines.
- **The Global Polio Eradication Initiative** (GPEI) — the WHO-coordinated programme that has reduced global polio cases from 350,000 per year in 1988 to 30 per year in 2024. The Gates Foundation has been the second-largest GPEI funder after the US government.
- **Malaria control programmes** — Gates Foundation funding for insecticide-treated bed nets, antimalarial drugs, and the principal malaria vaccine (RTS,S, subsequently approved by WHO for use in African children in 2021).
- **Tuberculosis control programmes** — Gates Foundation funding for the principal contemporary TB-vaccine-development programmes.

**The Gates Foundation has been one of the principal funders of major improvements in global child mortality.** Per the foundation's 2025 self-assessment, global under-five mortality has declined from 12 million deaths per year in 1990 to 4.9 million in 2024 — attributable in significant part to the foundation's funding of vaccination, antimalarial, and antiretroviral programmes. The Lancet's analysis documents similar findings.

The foundation has been **the second-largest funder of the World Health Organisation after the US government** since 2000. As of 2024, the foundation's WHO contributions are **$1.5 billion** — the same scale as US government contributions.

**The WHO's policy decisions have been influenced by the Gates Foundation's funding priorities** — heavy focus on vaccine-preventable infectious diseases, polio, malaria, and HIV, at the expense of the broader social-and-economic determinants of health the WHO had emphasised pre-2000. The critique, from former WHO senior officials and independent global-health researchers: **the Gates Foundation's funding has distorted the global public-health policy environment toward pharmaceutical-and-technological interventions and away from the social-determinants-of-health approach the historical WHO work had emphasised.**

### B. The COVID-19 period and the Imperial College modelling

The Gates Foundation has been a major funder of **Imperial College London's COVID-19 modelling work** through its long-standing support of Imperial's global-health programmes.

The **Imperial College COVID-19 Response Team** (led by **Professor Neil Ferguson**) produced the **16 March 2020 "Report 9"** — the modelling document that projected **510,000 deaths in the United Kingdom and 2.2 million deaths in the United States** in the absence of major non-pharmaceutical interventions (lockdowns). The Imperial College Report 9 modelling was the principal scientific document cited by the UK and US governments in their decisions to implement long lockdowns in March 2020.

The accuracy of the Imperial College projections has been contested in subsequent academic literature; the actual UK and US COVID-19 death tolls were much lower than the modelling projected. The defenders of the Imperial College modelling argue that the major reduction in death tolls was the consequence of the lockdown measures the modelling motivated; the critics argue that the underlying parameter assumptions had been pessimistic and that the policy response had been over-extended on the basis of the modelling.

The Gates Foundation's major funding of Imperial College's global-health programmes — and the Gates Foundation's subsequent funding of COVID-19 vaccine development through GAVI and CEPI — has been characterised by Gates Foundation critics as the case of **a foundation that funded both the modelling that produced the policy response and the products that addressed the policy response**. The accuracy of the critique is contested; the existence of the structural arrangement — Gates Foundation funding of both the modelling and the product development — is documented.

### C. The AGRA failure

The **Alliance for a Green Revolution in Africa (AGRA)** is **one of the most extensively documented operational failures of any major Gates Foundation programme**:

- **2006**: AGRA founded with Gates Foundation and Rockefeller Foundation funding. The principal Gates Foundation commitment across 2006-2024: **$1 billion**.
- AGRA's **stated goal**: to **double yields and incomes for 30 million small-scale farming families in Africa by 2020**, and to **halve food insecurity in 20 African countries**. The programme was framed as the African equivalent of the 1960s-1970s Asian "Green Revolution."
- **2014**: **Agnes Kalibata** appointed President of AGRA (a former Rwandan agriculture minister; she continues in the position through 2026).

The documented failure record:

- **July 2020**: The **Tufts University Global Development and Environment Institute** released **"Failing Africa's Farmers: An Impact Assessment of the Alliance for a Green Revolution in Africa"** (Timothy A. Wise). The principal findings:
  - **AGRA had not produced the doubled yields it had promised**.
  - **Crop diversity had declined** as AGRA promoted maize and other starchy crops at the expense of more nutritious indigenous crops.
  - **Food insecurity had not declined by half**; it had **increased by 31%** across the AGRA-target population.
  - **AGRA had refused to provide its own internal monitoring-and-evaluation data** to the Tufts researchers.
- **September 2021**: The **Alliance for Food Sovereignty in Africa (AFSA)** released an open letter signed by **200 African civil-society organisations** calling on the Gates Foundation to **withdraw its support for AGRA**.
- **June 2021**: African church leaders wrote directly to the Gates Foundation demanding changes. **The Gates Foundation did not respond** through 2026.
- **December 2021**: The Gates Foundation **donated an additional $40 million to AGRA** after the AFSA letter and the Tufts evaluation.
- **February 2022**: The **Mathematica evaluation** (commissioned by the Gates Foundation on behalf of AGRA's lead donors) confirmed the Tufts findings: AGRA had **not met its headline goal of increased incomes and food security**.
- **2020-2024**: AGRA quietly **removed its original headline goals from its website**.
- **August 2024**: African faith leaders released an open letter calling on the Gates Foundation to **pay reparations** for the ecological and social damages AGRA had produced.

**The principal Gates Foundation African agricultural development programme has, over its 18-year history, failed to produce the outcomes it promised, displaced traditional African seed-sovereignty practices, increased farmer debt, and been the subject of documented opposition from African civil-society organisations and faith leaders.** The Gates Foundation continued to fund the programme despite the documented failure record. AGRA is **one of the most consequential single cases of failure of the contemporary American billionaire philanthropy doctrine**.

### D. The Columbia Journalism Review investigation (2020)

In **August 2020**, the **Columbia Journalism Review (CJR)** published an investigation by Tim Schwab titled "**Journalism's Gates keepers**" that documented the Gates Foundation's major direct payments to major news organisations:

- The Gates Foundation has donated **$250 million directly to major news organisations** for journalism programmes over 2000-2020 — including **NPR ($24 million), the Guardian ($12.9 million), the Atlantic, the BBC, Le Monde, El País**, and various others.
- The CJR investigation documented the operational effect: **major news organisations that received Gates Foundation funding for journalism programmes under-reported critical perspectives on Gates Foundation programmes**.

**The captured-press framing of the Gates Foundation as a benevolent philanthropic actor was, in the CJR's analysis, produced by the Gates Foundation's direct funding of the journalism covering its activities.** The CJR investigation is **one of the most significant investigations of the financial relationships between a major US philanthropic foundation and the press that covers it**. Press response to the investigation has been limited; no subsequent examination of the foundation's media-funding relationships has followed.

### E. US K-12 education reform

The Gates Foundation has been **one of the principal private funders of US K-12 education reform** since 2000:

- The **Common Core State Standards Initiative** (2009-2014) — the principal contemporary US national-educational-standards programme. The Gates Foundation provided $200 million across 2008-2014 to fund Common Core's development, advocacy, and implementation. Common Core was presented as educator-led; the record establishes that **the foundation's funding was the principal financial driver of its adoption** across 45 US states.
- **Charter-school funding** — Gates Foundation funding for charter-school expansion during the post-2000 period.
- The **"Measures of Effective Teaching" project** (2009-2013) — a Gates Foundation programme to develop teacher-evaluation systems based on standardised-test scores. The project produced $45 million in research funding; its policy outcomes (the value-added teacher-evaluation systems adopted by 35 US states in 2009-2015) have been reversed across the post-2015 period as the accuracy and policy-effectiveness of value-added evaluation has been contested in the subsequent academic literature.

**The Gates Foundation's US K-12 education reform programmes have produced much less educational improvement than projected.** The post-2015 academic consensus: the foundation's K-12 reform doctrine — heavy reliance on standardised testing, charter-school expansion, and value-added teacher evaluation — has not produced systematic improvements in US student outcomes.

## XI. TerraPower, Breakthrough Energy, and Cascade Investment

### TerraPower

**TerraPower** — nuclear-energy company founded by Bill Gates in **2008**. Principal product: the **Natrium reactor**, a sodium-cooled fast-reactor design that the company is developing in collaboration with the **US Department of Energy** under the **Advanced Reactor Demonstration Program**.

**TerraPower's principal demonstration project** is being built at **Kemmerer, Wyoming**, on the site of a retiring coal-fired power plant. Construction began in 2024; the reactor is projected to begin operations in 2030. The federal support: $2 billion in Department of Energy funding has been provided for the demonstration reactor.

The Natrium reactor's safety and economic-viability claims have been contested; the underlying sodium-cooled fast-reactor design has been the subject of 60 years of development effort across multiple national programmes, none of which has produced a commercially competitive product. The operational outcome is, as of May 2026, **uncertain**.

### Breakthrough Energy

**Breakthrough Energy Ventures** — climate-technology investment fund founded by Gates in **2016**. Principal early investors: Bezos, Bloomberg, Branson, Ma, Andreessen Horowitz, and various other contemporary American billionaires. Initial fund size $1 billion; subsequent funds have brought total assets under management to **$3.5 billion** as of 2026.

**Breakthrough Energy Coalition** — the broader policy-advocacy programme; partners with national governments and the European Union on climate-technology research-and-development funding.

The Breakthrough Energy programme is **the principal contemporary American billionaire-funded climate-technology investment programme**. Operational outcomes are mixed; some portfolio companies have achieved major commercial scale (Form Energy, Boston Metal), while others have not.

### Cascade Investment

**Cascade Investment** — Bill Gates's principal personal investment vehicle; manages **$67 billion in assets** as of 2026. Cascade holdings include large positions in **Republic Services, Deere & Company, Canadian National Railway, FEMSA, and Crown Castle**, among others.

Cascade is **one of the largest single-family-office investment vehicles globally**. Managed by **Michael Larson** (Chief Investment Officer since 1994), Cascade has over 32 years produced returns that outperformed conventional market indices.

### The US farmland concentration

Through Cascade Investment, **Bill Gates is the largest single private owner of US farmland as of 2026**. Cascade-controlled entities own **270,000 acres across 18 US states**:

The Gates farmland holdings have been the subject of commentary on **structural consequences of senior-billionaire farmland concentration** — effects on agricultural-rent structures, displacement of family-farm ownership, and consolidation of US agricultural production into industrial-scale corporate operations. Gates's response: the Cascade holdings are conventional farmland-rental investments. The observation that the largest single US farmland holder is a non-farmer billionaire whose principal interest is portfolio diversification is accurate.

## XII. The 2025 Foundation wind-down announcement

On **8 May 2025**, Bill Gates announced that the Gates Foundation would **wind down its operations by 31 December 2045** — earlier than the previous plan to operate for 50 years after his death.

The announcement included the following commitments:

- **Total spending across the 2025-2045 period**: **$200 billion**, financed by:
  - The current foundation endowment of **$77 billion**.
  - Future Gates contributions (the Bloomberg-estimated $168 billion of his personal wealth as of May 2025).
  - Earnings from Gates's Breakthrough Energy and TerraPower investments.
- **Annual spending capacity 2025-2045**: **$9 billion per year** (compared to $6 billion per year that the foundation would have spent under the original "operate forever" structure).
- **Closure date**: **31 December 2045**.

Gates's verbatim characterisation of the decision (per his 8 May 2025 blog post): "**People will say a lot of things about me when I die, but I am determined that 'he died rich' will not be one of them. There are too many urgent problems to solve for me to hold onto resources that could be used to help people. That is why I have decided to give my money back to society much faster than I had originally planned.**"

The press framed the announcement as consequential. The May 2025 timing — **before the December 2025 Justice Department Epstein file releases** — has been the subject of subsequent commentary; that the announcement preceded the Epstein exposure is observable but not subject to specific motivational attribution.

The consequences of the 2045 wind-down:

- The foundation has **accelerated its grant-making rate** in 2025-2026; annual disbursement has increased from $6 billion to $9 billion.
- The **post-2045 absence of Gates Foundation funding** from the global public-health environment will produce serious gaps in vaccination, polio-eradication, malaria-control, and other programmes. The foundation has engaged in succession-planning conversations with the WHO, GAVI, CEPI, and other principal partners; the working outcomes are uncertain.
- **Gates's stated 99% wealth-reduction commitment** — his pledge to reduce his estimated $108-168 billion personal wealth by 99% across the 2025-2045 period — would, if executed, position him as the case of one of the most major personal-wealth-redistribution commitments in contemporary American billionaire history.

## XIII. Current position in 2026

This is what Bill Gates is, as of May 2026:

- The **co-founder of Microsoft** — the principal global personal-computer-and-enterprise-software platform, with 1.5 billion Windows-installed devices globally as of 2025 and a market capitalisation of $3.2 trillion.
- The **co-founder and operational principal of the Gates Foundation** — the largest private philanthropic foundation globally, with $77 billion in endowment and an $200 billion spending commitment across the 2025-2045 period.
- **One of the principal global public-health policy figures** — through the foundation's major funding of the WHO, GAVI, CEPI, the Global Polio Eradication Initiative, and the broader global vaccination-and-public-health infrastructure.
- **One of the principal climate-technology investment figures** — through Breakthrough Energy Ventures and TerraPower.
- The **largest single private owner of US farmland** — through Cascade Investment's 270,000-acre US farmland portfolio.
- The **subject of pending June 2026 congressional testimony on his relationship with Jeffrey Epstein**.
- The **subject of an ongoing Gates Foundation external investigation into the foundation's past engagement with Epstein**.
- The **subject of unverified-but-archived 2013 Epstein draft emails alleging major deceptive sexual conduct toward his ex-wife Melinda French Gates**.
- The **subject of the documented termination of the Warren Buffett-Gates Foundation funding relationship** subsequent to the December 2025 Epstein file releases.

**Forbes 2026 net worth**: **$108-115 billion** (declining from the May 2025 Bloomberg estimate of $168 billion as Gates has accelerated his foundation donations and as Microsoft share price has affected the Cascade holdings).

The biography is the form of **the senior contemporary American billionaire whose post-founder trajectory has been the construction of the largest private philanthropic vehicle in human history, and whose documented relationship with Jeffrey Epstein has produced one of the most consequential billionaire-personal-conduct scandals of the contemporary period**.
