## Lawrence Joseph Ellison

### Origins and family
- Born **17 August 1944, the Bronx, New York**.
- Mother: **Florence Spellman** — 19 years old, unmarried, of Russian-Jewish descent. Florence gave Larry up for adoption to her aunt and uncle when he was nine months old after he contracted pneumonia and Florence could not provide adequate care.
- Biological father: an Italian-American US Army Air Corps pilot whose name has not been publicly documented. Ellison has stated that he met his biological mother only once, when he was 48 years old.
- Adoptive parents: **Lillian "Lillie" and Louis Ellison** — Florence's aunt and uncle, who lived on the South Side of Chicago. Louis Ellison was a real-estate-agent immigrant from the Crimean region (then part of Russia, subsequently Ukraine). The Ellisons had immigrated through Ellis Island in the early 1900s and had taken the surname "Ellison" from Ellis Island.
- Louis Ellison lost his Chicago real-estate investments in the Depression; the family was, by the time of Larry's childhood, of modest economic position. Larry grew up in a two-bedroom apartment in the Jewish middle-class section of Chicago's South Shore neighbourhood.
- Reform-Jewish household. Bar mitzvah'd 1957.

### Education
- South Shore High School (Chicago public school).
- **University of Illinois at Urbana-Champaign** (1962-1964) — dropped out after his adoptive mother Lillie died of cancer in his sophomore year.
- **University of Chicago** (1966) — attended briefly; dropped out.
- No completed undergraduate degree.

### Pre-Oracle career
- Moved to California in 1966. Worked a series of programming jobs at the **Wells Fargo Bank**, **Fireman's Fund**, and **Amdahl Corporation**.
- **Ampex Corporation** (early-to-mid 1970s) — his first significant programming position. **Ampex's first major project for Ellison was a database for the CIA, code-named "Oracle"** — the project name that Ellison would subsequently take for his own company.

### Oracle founding (1977)
- In 1977, Ellison co-founded **Software Development Laboratories (SDL)** with **Bob Miner** (Ellison's former supervisor at Ampex) and **Ed Oates**. Initial capital: $2,000 (of which $1,200 was Ellison's).
- Ellison was inspired by **Edgar F. Codd's 1970 paper "A Relational Model of Data for Large Shared Data Banks"** — the IBM research paper that established the theoretical foundation of relational-database systems. Ellison's key insight was that **IBM had not yet commercialised the relational-database model**, leaving the commercial opportunity open for a startup that could implement it.
- The company was renamed **Relational Software, Inc.** in 1979, then **Oracle Systems Corporation** in 1982, then **Oracle Corporation** in 1995.
- **CEO 1977-2014**. **Executive Chairman and CTO since 2014**.

### Oracle's principal trajectory
- 1977-1986: early period, growth from startup to public company. Oracle IPO 12 March 1986 (one day before Microsoft's IPO).
- 1986-1990s: aggressive expansion. The company was nearly destroyed by an accounting scandal in 1990 (revenue-recognition irregularities; Ellison subsequently rebuilt the company).
- 2005: **Acquired PeopleSoft for $10.3 billion** in a hostile takeover. The PeopleSoft acquisition was Ellison's aggressive corporate-warfare doctrine.
- 2010: **Acquired Sun Microsystems for $7.4 billion**. The Sun acquisition gave Oracle the Java programming language and the MySQL database; both were subsequently used to extract licensing fees from the broader technology industry.
- 2014: Ellison stepped down as CEO; **Mark Hurd and Safra Catz became co-CEOs**. Hurd died in October 2019; Catz remains as sole CEO.
- 2010s: Oracle's competitive position sharply declined through the cloud-computing transition — the company missed the principal cloud-computing wave (AWS, Azure, Google Cloud) and was, by the late 2010s, characterised as "the legacy database vendor."

### The 2024-2025 AI pivot and the Stargate participation
- **September 2024**: Oracle's stock began a rally based on the company's announced positioning as a principal AI-infrastructure provider.
- **21 January 2025** (one day after Trump's inauguration): Ellison appeared at the **White House with Sam Altman (OpenAI) and Masayoshi Son (SoftBank) to announce the Stargate Project** — the $500 billion AI-infrastructure investment programme described in Chapter 13. Trump's verbatim characterisation of Ellison at the announcement: "**In the case of Larry, it's well beyond technology, he's sort of CEO of everything.**"
- **September 2025**: Oracle stock jumped 40% in a single day on the announcement of a **$300 billion deal with OpenAI for computing power**. The deal made OpenAI Oracle's largest customer. **Ellison's wealth increased by $89 billion in a single day** — the largest single-day wealth increase ever documented by the Bloomberg Billionaires Index.
- **10 September 2025**: Ellison briefly became **the wealthiest person in the world**, with estimated net worth of **$393 billion**. He subsequently fell back to second/third position behind Musk and Zuckerberg as Oracle stock corrected.
- **2026**: President Trump appointed Ellison to the **President's Council of Advisors on Science and Technology (PCAST)**.

### Oracle's federal-contracting positions
- Long-standing federal contractor since the company's 1970s founding (the original CIA "Oracle" project, the company's name origin).
- **TikTok divestiture deal (2025)**: Oracle obtained a minority stake in the US TikTok operations as part of the divestiture mandated by the ByteDance-divestiture legislation. The deal was negotiated through Ellison's direct Trump relationship.
- Various Department of Defense and intelligence-community cloud contracts; Oracle is one of the four principal JWCC vendors (alongside AWS, Microsoft Azure, and Google Cloud).

### The Skydance-Paramount acquisition
- David Ellison (Larry's son, born 1983) founded **Skydance Media** in 2010 — initial capital from Larry Ellison.
- **January 2024**: Skydance first bid for Paramount Global.
- **August 2024**: Skydance and Paramount announced merger; largely financed by Larry Ellison's family trust.
- **2024-2025**: Lengthy regulatory-review process. Trump's lawsuit against CBS News over a Kamala Harris interview was settled by Paramount for **$16 million in July 2025** — settlement that was widely characterised as a portfolio-management decision to clear the regulatory path for the Skydance acquisition.
- **August 2025**: Paramount-Skydance merger closed. **The Ellison family trust became the largest shareholder of the new Paramount Skydance entity**; David Ellison became operational head. The combined entity controls CBS News, Paramount Pictures, MTV, Nickelodeon, Showtime, the Paramount+ streaming service.
- **September 2025**: Paramount Skydance hired **Bari Weiss** (founder of The Free Press) as editor-in-chief of CBS News. The Weiss appointment was the platform's ideological repositioning.
- **October-December 2025**: Paramount Skydance bid for Warner Bros. Discovery ($111 billion offer, including Larry Ellison's personal financing). Warner Bros. Discovery rejected the bid in favour of Netflix's offer.
- **November 2025**: Paramount Skydance acquired exclusive media rights to UFC — the case of the Ellison-Dana White operational alignment.

### Political activity
- Through 2012: bipartisan donor (donated to both parties).
- **Since 2016**: Republican-aligned. **One of the top donors ($4 million by February 2016)** to **Conservative Solutions PAC**, the super PAC supporting Marco Rubio's 2016 presidential campaign.
- **2020 election**: per *Washington Post* (May 2022), Ellison **participated in a conference call days after the 2020 election that focused on strategies for challenging the legitimacy of the vote**. Other participants on the call included **Sean Hannity (Fox News), Senator Lindsey Graham, Jay Sekulow (Trump personal attorney), and James Bopp (attorney for True the Vote)**. The Washington Post cited court documents and a participant on the call.
- **2024 cycle**: major donations to Trump campaign and Trump-aligned PACs.
- **2025**: ongoing role as principal Trump-administration AI-policy advisor; PCAST appointment.

### Personal life
- **Marriages**: Four times. First wife Adda Quinn (1967-1974); second wife Nancy Wheeler Jenkins (1977-1978); third wife Barbara Boothe (1983-1986; mother of David and Megan); fourth wife Melanie Craft (2003-2010); fifth wife Nikita Kahn (2015-2020). Currently married to **Jolin (Keren) Ellison** (née Zhu, alumna of University of Michigan); they have four children.
- **Children**: David Ellison (b. 1983) — Skydance / Paramount Skydance CEO; Megan Ellison (b. 1986) — founder of Annapurna Pictures; four younger children with Jolin.
- **Properties**: owns **98% of the Hawaiian island of Lānaʻi** (acquired June 2012 for $300 million). Has owned the island for 14 years and has executed major development projects there. Major Malibu real estate; the Porcupine Creek estate in Rancho Mirage, California; multiple yachts including the 192-foot *Musashi*.

### Donations and ideology
- **$16.6 million donation to the Friends of the Israel Defense Forces (FIDF) in 2017** — the largest donation in the organisation's history. Ellison's verbatim statement: "Since Israel's founding, we have called on the brave men and women of the IDF to defend our home."
- Long-standing public position critical of Edward Snowden: "Snowden had yet to identify a single person who had been 'wrongly injured' by the NSA's data collection."
- Has advocated publicly for what he has characterised as a "surveillance society" — a quotation from an Oracle investor presentation: "**Citizens will be on their best behavior, because we're constantly recording and reporting everything that's going on.**"

### Current position
- **Forbes 2026 net worth**: **$201-203 billion** (sixth-richest globally as of early 2026; Oracle stock sharply declined from the September 2025 peak).
- **Oracle Executive Chairman and CTO**.
- **Paramount Skydance controlling shareholder**.
- **PCAST member**.
- **President's Council of Advisors on Science and Technology member**.

---

## Laurence Douglas "Larry" Fink

### Origins and family
- Born **2 November 1952, Los Angeles, California**.
- Father: **Frederick Fink** — owner of a shoe store in Van Nuys, California.
- Mother: **Lila Fink** — English professor at California State University, Northridge.
- Jewish family. Raised in **Van Nuys** (San Fernando Valley, Los Angeles).

### Education
- Birmingham High School (Van Nuys).
- **University of California, Los Angeles (UCLA)**: BA Political Science 1974; MBA in Real Estate 1976. Fink has, in subsequent statements, described his UCLA MBA in Real Estate as prefiguring his subsequent career — he was, throughout his early career, a fixed-income-securities specialist with expertise in mortgage-backed securities.

### Pre-BlackRock career (1976-1988)
- **First Boston** (1976-1988): Joined as a bond trader; rose to become **Head of the Taxable Bond Division and a member of the Management Committee**. At First Boston, Fink helped pioneer the **mortgage-backed securities (MBS) market** — the financial instruments that would, in the 1980s and 2000s, become foundational to American consumer-mortgage finance.
- **1986**: Fink's bond-trading desk lost **$100 million in a single quarter** through an interest-rate hedging mistake. The loss ended his First Boston career.
- The 1986 loss is, in Fink's subsequent biographical statements, the foundational professional formation of his subsequent career — the experience that motivated the founding of BlackRock around the principle of comprehensive risk management.

### BlackRock founding (1988)
- **1988**: Fink co-founded **BlackRock** with **Robert Kapito, Susan Wagner, Barbara Novick, Ben Golub, Hugh Frater, Ralph Schlosstein, and Keith Anderson** within **Blackstone Group** as a subsidiary.
- Initial focus: fixed-income asset management with sophisticated risk-management technology.
- **1994**: BlackRock split from Blackstone (the family-name origins: Blackstone retained "stone"; the new firm took "rock") after Fink and Stephen Schwarzman (Blackstone CEO) disagreed over equity compensation structure. Fink subsequently described the split as "a divorce."
- **1999**: BlackRock IPO on the NYSE at $14 per share.

### BlackRock's expansion (1999-2024)
- **2006**: Acquired **Merrill Lynch Investment Managers** for $9.8 billion. The acquisition doubled BlackRock's AUM.
- **2008-2009 financial crisis**: BlackRock was the **principal contractor for the Federal Reserve's and Treasury's emergency-asset-management programmes**. The firm managed the wind-down of Bear Stearns ($30 billion in assets), AIG ($180 billion in assets), and various other crisis-period portfolios. The 2008-2009 federal contracts were, the foundational government-relationship event of BlackRock's subsequent position.
- **June 2009**: Acquired **Barclays Global Investors** for $13.5 billion. The acquisition brought **iShares** — the principal ETF (exchange-traded fund) franchise globally. The iShares acquisition transformed BlackRock from a fixed-income firm into the principal global passive-investment manager.
- **2010s**: Sustained AUM growth through the global passive-investment expansion. BlackRock's AUM passed $1 trillion in 2009, $4 trillion in 2014, $6 trillion in 2017, $8 trillion in 2020.
- **2024**: Acquired **Global Infrastructure Partners (GIP)** for **$12.5 billion** — the case of BlackRock's expansion into private-infrastructure investment. The GIP acquisition gave BlackRock control of global airport, port, pipeline, and energy infrastructure assets.
- **2024**: Acquired **HPS Investment Partners** for $12 billion (private credit). The HPS acquisition gave BlackRock private-credit capacity.
- **2025**: Acquired **Preqin** (private-markets data) for $3.2 billion.

### Current AUM and operational scale (May 2026)
- **BlackRock AUM**: **$12.5 trillion as of Q1 2026** — by far the largest asset manager globally; twice the AUM of the second-largest firm (Vanguard at $9 trillion).
- The figure represents **10% of all global investable assets**. BlackRock is, the **principal global passive-investment infrastructure**; large fractions of every major listed company globally are held by BlackRock-managed funds.
- **Aladdin platform**: BlackRock's proprietary risk-management software. Aladdin is used by **20,000 portfolio managers globally** (including large fractions of the world's largest pension funds, sovereign-wealth funds, central banks, and private-investment firms). Aladdin manages **$20 trillion in third-party assets** in addition to BlackRock's own $12.5 trillion.
- The combined Aladdin-managed-plus-BlackRock-managed asset pool is **$32 trillion** — that is, **25% of all global investable assets pass through BlackRock's risk-management infrastructure**.

### The ESG controversy and reversal
- **2018-2020**: Fink's annual letters to CEOs articulated the **"stakeholder capitalism" doctrine** — the proposition that corporate decision-making should consider environmental, social, and governance (ESG) factors alongside shareholder returns. The 2020 letter was particularly emphatic on climate-related risk.
- **2021-2023**: Republican-aligned political response. Multiple Republican state attorneys general (Florida's James Uthmeier, Texas's Ken Paxton, others) accused BlackRock of "violating fiduciary duty" through ESG investment policies. Multiple Republican-controlled state pension funds (Texas, Florida, Tennessee, others) withdrew $4 billion in total from BlackRock.
- **2024**: BlackRock reversed its ESG positioning. The 2024 annual letter abandoned the "stakeholder capitalism" framing; BlackRock withdrew from the **Climate Action 100+** investor coalition (February 2024) and from various other ESG-related industry initiatives.
- **2025-2026**: Fink's annual letters have emphasised AI infrastructure, infrastructure investment, and the "tokenisation of every asset" — the turn away from ESG and toward the new-world-order capital-deployment programme.

### The AI Infrastructure Partnership (AIP) and the OpenAI-Microsoft-Nvidia integration
- **September 2024**: BlackRock, **Global Infrastructure Partners**, **MGX (UAE sovereign-wealth fund)**, and **Microsoft** announced the **AI Infrastructure Partnership (AIP)** — a $30 billion initial fund commitment for AI-infrastructure investment.
- **March 2025**: AIP expanded to include **NVIDIA and xAI** (Musk's AI company). The expanded AIP was framed as the principal global AI-infrastructure capital channel.
- **October 2025**: BlackRock announced a **$40 billion AI data-centre investment partnership** with Microsoft and the **MGX UAE sovereign-wealth fund**. The deal was the structural integration of BlackRock's passive-investment infrastructure with the OpenAI-Microsoft-Stargate AI buildout.
- The combined AIP-plus-Stargate-plus-AI-data-centre capital deployment is **$600-700 billion across the 2025-2029 period** — the largest single capital-deployment programme in contemporary American history outside the federal budget.

### Other BlackRock structural positions
- **Stewardship voting**: BlackRock's iShares-and-other-ETF holdings produce **$3 trillion in voting rights at S&P 500 companies**. BlackRock's stewardship team (60 staff) executes the proxy votes; the votes are, the principal mechanism by which BlackRock influences the boards and policies of the principal global corporations.
- **Central bank relationships**: BlackRock is a contractor to multiple central banks. The Federal Reserve uses BlackRock for emergency-asset-management programmes (the 2020 COVID-19 Federal Reserve corporate-bond-purchase programme was managed by BlackRock). The European Central Bank, Bank of Japan, and various other central banks have BlackRock advisory relationships.
- **Sovereign-wealth-fund relationships**: BlackRock manages large portions of the Norwegian Government Pension Fund Global, the Saudi Public Investment Fund, the Abu Dhabi Investment Authority, the China Investment Corporation, and most of the other principal sovereign-wealth funds globally.

### Political activity and bipartisan integration
- Fink has, throughout his career, been bipartisan in political activity. Donated to both parties; serves on policy councils under both Republican and Democratic administrations.
- **2008**: Considered as Treasury Secretary by both McCain and Obama transition teams.
- **2016**: Considered as Treasury Secretary by Trump transition team. Did not take the position.
- **2024-2025**: Closely aligned with the second Trump administration's AI-infrastructure-investment programme. The BlackRock-AIP-Stargate operational integration is the evidence of the alignment.
- **President's Council of Advisors on Science and Technology** — appointed 2025.

### Personal life
- Married **Lori Fink** (née Friedman) since 1974. Three children: Joshua Fink (founder of Enso Capital Management), Alexander Fink, and Elizabeth Fink.
- Lives in New York City. Sizeable homes in Aspen, Colorado, and North Salem, New York.

### Current position
- **Forbes 2026 net worth**: **$2.5-3 billion** (much smaller than the senior-billionaire technology class because Fink's wealth is primarily in BlackRock equity and the firm's executive-compensation pattern).
- **Chairman and CEO of BlackRock**.
- **Manager of 25% of all global investable assets** through the combined BlackRock-AUM and Aladdin-managed-third-party assets.
- **Principal capital-deployment coordinator** for the AI Infrastructure Partnership and the broader new-world-order AI-buildout programme.

# Sources — Chapter 19: Bill Gates

All claims verified 13 May 2026.

## Origins and family

- **William Henry Gates III**: born **28 October 1955**, Seattle, Washington.
- **Father**: **William Henry Gates Sr.** (1925-2020) — prominent Seattle attorney, founding partner of **Preston Gates & Ellis** (subsequently merged to become K&L Gates, one of the principal US law firms). Served in the US Army during World War II. Significant figure in Seattle civic life across seven decades.
- **Mother**: **Mary Maxwell Gates** (1929-1994) — University of Washington graduate; schoolteacher. **Member of the board of First Interstate Bancsystem and of the board of directors of IBM** (1980-1993). **Mary Gates also served on the board of United Way of America**; through this position she was personally acquainted with **John Opel, the IBM CEO** during the IBM-Microsoft contract negotiations of 1980-1981. Mary Gates's IBM-board position is the documented connection that materially altered the trajectory of the 1980 IBM-Microsoft operating-system contract.
- **Siblings**: older sister **Kristianne (Kristi) Gates Blake** (born 1953), and younger sister **Libby Gates Armintrout** (born 1964).
- The Gates family was, the senior Seattle Anglo-Protestant professional family of the post-1945 period — more wealthy and more institutionally networked than the Andreessen rural-Wisconsin family or the Zuckerberg Dobbs-Ferry family.

## Education

- **Lakeside School** (private school, Seattle, 1967-1973). Lakeside was, in 1968, one of the very few US private schools to have computer-time-sharing access; the school's "Mothers' Club" purchased a Teletype terminal connected via timeshare to a General Electric computer. Gates and his Lakeside classmate **Paul Allen** developed programming experience on the Lakeside terminal across five years.
- **Harvard University** (1973-1975). Entered as pre-law; switched to mathematics and computer science. **Dropped out in his sophomore year (1975)** to focus on Microsoft. Harvard awarded Gates an **honorary degree** in June 2007, at which Gates delivered the commencement address.

## Pre-Microsoft companies

- **Traf-O-Data** (1972) — Gates's first business venture, founded with Paul Allen and Paul Gilbert. The company developed a small computer based on the Intel 8008 microprocessor that read raw data from traffic-counting machines and produced reports for local governments. The company produced $20,000 in revenue across its lifetime; it ceased operations in 1980 as the underlying technology became obsolete. Traf-O-Data is the Gates-Allen early partnership; the active competence the two developed in the Traf-O-Data work is the foundation of the subsequent Microsoft venture.

## Microsoft founding

- **4 April 1975**: Microsoft (originally "Micro-Soft") founded by Bill Gates and Paul Allen in **Albuquerque, New Mexico** — proximate to MITS, the company that produced the **Altair 8800** microcomputer for which Gates and Allen wrote their first commercial product (the **Microsoft Altair BASIC** interpreter).
- The Altair BASIC interpreter was developed in six weeks in early 1975; Gates and Allen wrote it without ever having seen an Altair computer (they used a PDP-10 emulator). The interpreter was the first commercial Microsoft product.
- Microsoft moved its operations from Albuquerque to Bellevue, Washington (suburban Seattle) in 1979.

## The 1980 IBM contract and the QDOS / 86-DOS / MS-DOS acquisition

The most operationally consequential single event of Microsoft's history was the **November 1980 contract with IBM** to provide the operating system for the **IBM Personal Computer** (PC), which IBM launched in August 1981.

The documented sequence:

1. **Mid-1980**: IBM, developing its PC project, contacts Microsoft seeking a BASIC programming language for the PC. Microsoft had become the dominant supplier of BASIC interpreters across the early-microcomputer industry.
2. **July 1980**: IBM also seeks an operating system for the PC. **Gates referred IBM to Digital Research, Inc. (DRI)** — the company owned by **Gary Kildall** that produced the **CP/M operating system**, then the dominant microcomputer operating system.
3. **July 1980**: IBM representatives visited DRI's offices in Pacific Grove, California. **Gary Kildall was reportedly absent** (the evidence is disputed; Kildall maintained he had flown to the Bay Area for legitimate business; Gates and IBM officials maintained Kildall had "gone flying" — that is, was personally flying his airplane recreationally). His wife **Dorothy Kildall (née McEwen)** declined to sign IBM's non-disclosure agreement on the first meeting. The consequence: **the IBM-DRI negotiation broke down** before any operating-system licensing agreement was reached.
4. **August 1980**: IBM returned to Microsoft and asked Gates to supply an operating system for the PC.
5. **Microsoft did not have an operating system**. Gates and Allen knew of **Tim Paterson**, a programmer at **Seattle Computer Products (SCP)** in Tukwila, Washington, who had developed **86-DOS** (originally named **QDOS**, for "**Quick and Dirty Operating System**") — a 16-bit operating system Paterson had built to address the absence of an 8086-compatible version of CP/M. **86-DOS was, per multiple subsequent analyses and per a 1982 Digital Research lawsuit, modeled on CP/M's command structure and application programming interface.**
6. **December 1980**: Microsoft licensed 86-DOS from SCP for a **non-exclusive royalty fee of $25,000**.
7. **6 November 1980**: Microsoft signed the agreement with IBM to provide the operating system for the IBM PC. The agreement structure was Gates's contracting acumen:
   - Microsoft would deliver the operating system to IBM at a low flat fee ($50,000-$75,000 across the deal's lifetime).
   - **Microsoft retained the right to license the operating system to other manufacturers**. This was the operationally consequential provision; when IBM PC clones subsequently emerged (Compaq, Dell, others), Microsoft was positioned to license MS-DOS to all of them.
   - IBM had offered $450,000 for an exclusive license; Microsoft negotiated the much lower $50,000-$75,000 in exchange for non-exclusivity.
8. **May 1981**: Microsoft hired Tim Paterson directly.
9. **July 1981**: Microsoft purchased **all rights to 86-DOS from SCP for $50,000** (some sources cite $75,000 depending on how royalty payments are calculated). Microsoft renamed 86-DOS as **MS-DOS** for non-IBM licensees and **PC-DOS** for IBM.
10. **12 August 1981**: IBM launched the IBM PC with PC-DOS as its operating system.

### The Mary Gates / John Opel / IBM CEO connection

The documented record establishes that **Mary Gates personally knew John Opel, the IBM CEO** during the 1980-1981 IBM-Microsoft contract negotiations. **Mary Gates served on the United Way of America's executive board** alongside Opel; the two had a long-standing personal relationship through United Way activity.

The documented anecdote (recorded in multiple subsequent biographies and corroborated by IBM executives who were present at the relevant period): when **John Opel was first informed that IBM would be working with Microsoft as its operating-system supplier**, his reported response was: "**Oh, is that Mary Gates' boy's company?**" The verbatim Opel quotation is documented in *Hard Drive: Bill Gates and the Making of the Microsoft Empire* (James Wallace and Jim Erickson, John Wiley & Sons, 1992) and in subsequent investigative work.

The observation: **the most operationally consequential single contract in Microsoft's history — the November 1980 IBM PC operating-system contract — was facilitated by the personal relationship between Mary Gates and the IBM CEO**. The captured-press framing of Microsoft's founding as a "garage startup" elides this documented family-network connection at the working core of the company's foundational contract.

### The Digital Research lawsuit aftermath

When Digital Research engineers obtained access to MS-DOS in 1982, they identified strong similarities to CP/M. Gary Kildall reportedly demonstrated to **science-fiction writer Jerry Pournelle** that DOS contained CP/M code by entering a command in DOS that displayed Kildall's name (the accuracy of the Pournelle anecdote is disputed; Pournelle never revealed the specific command and no subsequent investigation has independently corroborated it).

Digital Research threatened legal action against IBM. IBM settled by agreeing to also distribute CP/M-86 for the IBM PC; CP/M-86 was priced at $240, six times the $40 price of PC-DOS. The pricing differential eliminated CP/M-86 from the IBM PC market; PC-DOS became the dominant operating system by default.

**Gary Kildall died in July 1994** under circumstances that have remained contested; documented indicates a fall in a Monterey bar that produced a head injury from which he died three days later, at age 52. Kildall's documented contribution to the foundation of personal computing has been under-recognised relative to Gates's; the observation that the dominant operating-system platform of the contemporary period emerged from the **extensive copying of Kildall's CP/M into Paterson's QDOS, the Microsoft acquisition of QDOS, and the subsequent licensing of the result as MS-DOS** is one of the more documented cases of the "founder-myth-construction" pattern the book has identified.

## Microsoft's principal trajectory (1981-2000)

- **1981-1985**: MS-DOS becomes the dominant operating system as the IBM PC and the IBM PC clone market expands. Microsoft's revenue grows from $16 million in 1981 to $140 million in 1985.
- **20 November 1985**: **Microsoft Windows 1.0** released. Windows is initially a graphical-shell layer on top of MS-DOS rather than a separate operating system.
- **13 March 1986**: **Microsoft IPO** at $21 per share. Closing first-day price: $27.75. Market capitalisation at IPO: $520 million. Bill Gates personally retained 45% of Microsoft's shares; **by year-end 1986 he was the youngest billionaire in history at age 31**.
- **1990s**: Dominant Windows position. Windows 3.0 (1990), Windows 95 (1995), Windows 98 (1998), Windows 2000 (2000), Windows XP (2001).
- **22 May 1990**: Microsoft Office released.
- **24 August 1995**: Windows 95 launched in a marketing event including the Rolling Stones' "Start Me Up" as the launch song (reportedly licensed for $14 million). The launch is one of the most operationally significant single product launches in technology-industry history.

## US v. Microsoft antitrust litigation (1998-2001)

- **18 May 1998**: The **US Department of Justice and 20 state attorneys general filed an antitrust lawsuit against Microsoft**. The principal allegation: Microsoft had **abused its monopoly position in personal-computer operating systems** to extend the monopoly to web browsers (by bundling Internet Explorer with Windows in a manner designed to eliminate Netscape Navigator).
- The trial was held before **US District Judge Thomas Penfield Jackson** in late 1998 and 1999. Gates's videotaped deposition was widely characterised in the trade press as evasive; Jackson personally noted in subsequent commentary that he had found Gates's testimony "**inherently without credibility**."
- **5 November 1999**: Jackson issued his **Findings of Fact**, ruling that Microsoft had **monopoly power in the operating-system market and had used that power against competitors**.
- **3 April 2000**: Jackson issued his **Conclusions of Law**, ruling that Microsoft had **violated Sections 1 and 2 of the Sherman Antitrust Act**.
- **7 June 2000**: Jackson issued his **Final Judgment**, ordering the **break-up of Microsoft into two companies** — one for operating systems and one for application software.
- **28 June 2001**: The US Court of Appeals for the DC Circuit **reversed the break-up order** (while upholding Jackson's findings on antitrust violations) and removed Jackson from the case (citing his out-of-court press interviews during the trial).
- **November 2001**: The case settled. Microsoft agreed to **share its APIs with third-party developers and to permit PC manufacturers to bundle non-Microsoft software** — narrower remedies than the break-up Jackson had ordered.
- The settlement is the most operationally significant antitrust action against a US technology company between AT&T (1984) and Google (2024); the consequence of the limited remedy was that **Microsoft retained its monopoly position in personal-computer operating systems through the subsequent twenty-five years**.

## CEO and Chairman transitions

- **13 January 2000**: Gates **stepped down as CEO**; **Steve Ballmer became CEO**. Gates remained Chairman and "Chief Software Architect."
- **27 June 2008**: Gates **stepped back from day-to-day Microsoft operations** to focus on the Gates Foundation.
- **4 February 2014**: Gates **stepped down as Chairman**. **John W. Thompson became Chairman**; **Satya Nadella became CEO** (succeeding Steve Ballmer).
- **13 March 2020**: Gates **resigned from the Microsoft board of directors** (and also from the Berkshire Hathaway board). The resignation was, in the contemporary press framing, presented as part of the broader Gates Foundation focus; the subsequent *Wall Street Journal* reporting (May 2021) documented that **the board resignation followed an internal Microsoft investigation into Gates's relationship with a female Microsoft employee** — Microsoft board members had been concerned about the relationship and had pressed Gates to resign.

## The Bill and Melinda Gates Foundation

### Origins and structure

- **William H. Gates Foundation** (1994-2000), focused on global health and Pacific Northwest community programmes; managed by William H. Gates Sr.
- **Gates Library Foundation** (1997), subsequently renamed **Gates Learning Foundation**, focused on Internet access to public libraries.
- **2000**: The two foundations merged to create the **Bill and Melinda Gates Foundation**. The foundation has been, throughout the post-2000 period, **the largest private philanthropic foundation globally**.
- **Endowment as of 2025**: **$77 billion**. Cumulative grants disbursed through 2024: **$78 billion**. Annual grant-making capacity (2024-2025): **$9 billion per year**.

### Foundation leadership

- **Co-founders**: Bill Gates and Melinda French Gates.
- **Chairman of the board**: William H. Gates Sr. (until his death in 2020).
- **CEO 2000-2008**: **Patty Stonesifer** (subsequently a member of the **Amazon board**, Chapter 16). The Stonesifer-Bezos board connection is one of the documented operational links between the Gates Foundation and the Bezos network.
- **CEO 2008-2014**: **Jeff Raikes** (former Microsoft executive).
- **CEO 2014-2020**: **Sue Desmond-Hellmann** (previously Chancellor of UC San Francisco; **subsequently a member of the OpenAI board**). The Desmond-Hellmann-Altman-Bezos-Gates network connection is one of the documented operational links between the contemporary AI industry and the global-philanthropic-public-health network.
- **CEO 2020-present**: **Mark Suzman**.
- **2024**: **Melinda French Gates resigned as co-chair** of the foundation (subsequently to her 2021 divorce from Bill Gates). She received a clean separation from the foundation funded by Bill Gates personally (estimated $12.5 billion). The foundation was renamed from "Bill and Melinda Gates Foundation" to simply the "**Gates Foundation**."

### Principal Gates Foundation activities

- **Global health**: vaccination programmes (GAVI, the Vaccine Alliance — co-founded 2000), the Global Polio Eradication Initiative, malaria-control programmes, tuberculosis-control programmes, HIV/AIDS programmes.
- **CEPI** (Coalition for Epidemic Preparedness Innovations) — co-founded 2017 at the World Economic Forum in Davos; Gates Foundation funding for pandemic-vaccine preparedness; CEPI was the principal funder of the Oxford-AstraZeneca COVID-19 vaccine and several other major COVID-19 vaccines.
- **Agricultural development**: the **Alliance for a Green Revolution in Africa (AGRA)**, co-founded 2006 with the Rockefeller Foundation; principal Gates Foundation programme for African agricultural development. AGRA's documented failure is treated in detail below.
- **Education**: US K-12 education reform programmes; the **Common Core State Standards Initiative** (the controversial early-2010s national educational-standards programme that was largely funded by the Gates Foundation); charter-school funding.
- **Climate**: the Breakthrough Energy Coalition, the Gates Foundation Climate Programme.

### Warren Buffett's donations

**Warren Buffett pledged in 2006 to donate all of his Berkshire Hathaway shares to philanthropic causes** — 83% to the Gates Foundation and the remainder to four foundations associated with his children. Through 2024, Buffett had donated **$43.3 billion to the Gates Foundation**.

In June 2024, Buffett amended his estate plan to direct that after his death, his remaining Berkshire shares would NOT go to the Gates Foundation but to a new foundation administered by his children. The Buffett-Gates relationship has been affected by the 2026 Justice Department Epstein file releases (see below); per April 2026 reporting, **Buffett has not spoken to Gates since the Epstein disclosures came to light** and has suggested he may not make further donations.

## The Jeffrey Epstein relationship

The most operationally significant single biographical fact about Bill Gates that the press framing has under-reported is documented record of his relationship with **Jeffrey Epstein** — the convicted sex offender who died in federal custody on **10 August 2019**.

### The documented relationship

The major record (per multiple *New York Times*, *Wall Street Journal*, *Fortune*, and CNN investigations across the period 2019-2026):

- **Gates met Epstein multiple times** at Epstein's Manhattan townhouse and other locations across the period 2011-2019. At least **three of the meetings were held at Epstein's "palatial Manhattan townhouse"** (per the New York Times 2019 reporting); Gates **stayed late into the night** at the townhouse on at least one occasion.
- Gates and Epstein **discussed philanthropy and the Gates Foundation extensively**; advisors to Gates frequently forwarded Epstein emails about their work.
- Epstein **pitched Gates on a "donor-advised fund"** that would manage the wealth of newly-minted billionaires; Epstein hoped to obtain a position-of-influence over the philanthropic structure of the contemporary American billionaire class. **Gates declined to participate in the donor-advised fund** (the documented internal Gates-team communications indicate that **Melinda French Gates personally opposed her husband's participation**).
- **Melinda French Gates met Epstein with Bill Gates in 2013** and was, per multiple subsequent reports, uncomfortable with the relationship. She told Bill that she was uncomfortable with Epstein after the 2013 meeting; Bill Gates and other Gates Foundation employees nevertheless continued to maintain the relationship with Epstein through 2019.

### The intermediaries network

Per the **Fortune** investigation (March 2026) based on the **Justice Department's 3-million-page Epstein document release**, Epstein had constructed a network of intermediaries to maintain his relationship with Gates:

- **Boris Nikolic** — Gates's "right hand" and chief science advisor for periods of 2008-2020. Nikolic was a Croatian-born oncologist who became one of Gates's principal scientific advisors at the Gates Foundation and at Gates's personal venture-investment vehicle bgC3. **Epstein and Nikolic communicated as late as 2019**. Nikolic told *Fortune* in March 2026 that "**Epstein inserted himself as a mediator**" into Nikolic's separation from Gates's venture fund "**and then used lies to pursue his own agenda**."
- **Melanie Walker** — former Gates Foundation senior advisor. Walker has declined to comment on the documented Epstein communications; she has not been charged with wrongdoing.
- **Mila Antonova** — Russian citizen who has been characterised in the Fortune reporting as a former Bill Gates mistress. Epstein helped Antonova secure a US visa, hosted her in his Manhattan apartments, funded her coding-school education, and sent her wire transfers. **Epstein subsequently used the favours he had provided Antonova to attempt to pressure Gates into supporting the donor-advised fund**.

### The 2013 draft emails and the alleged STI / antibiotics claim

The **December 2025 Justice Department Epstein file release** included **two draft emails Epstein appears to have written to himself in July 2013**. The emails were never sent; they were stream-of-consciousness notes saved in Epstein's email account.

In the draft emails, **Epstein appears to claim that he facilitated sexual encounters for Gates and helped Gates obtain medication to hide a sexually transmitted infection from his wife Melinda**. The draft emails include the following content (per CNN and NPR reporting, February 2026):

- Epstein writes that Gates asked him for help "**in order to deal with consequences of sex with russian girls**" and "**illicit trysts, with married women**."
- Epstein writes that Gates asked him to provide **Adderall for bridge tournaments**.
- Epstein writes that Gates "**tearfully**" asked him to delete messages referencing the STI, "**your request that I provide you antibiotics that you can surreptitiously give to Melinda**," and the description of Gates's penis.

**The draft emails are unverified and uncorroborated**. There is no indication the emails were ever sent to Gates or anyone else. **Gates has not been accused of any criminal wrongdoing related to Epstein**. A spokesman for Gates told *Fortune* in March 2026 that Gates "**regrets meeting with Epstein**" and that he "**never witnessed or engaged in any illicit or illegal behavior**."

### The Melinda French Gates divorce

- **3 May 2021**: Bill and Melinda Gates announced their divorce after 27 years of marriage.
- Per the **Wall Street Journal** reporting (10 May 2021), **Melinda Gates had been meeting with divorce lawyers since at least 2019, when the New York Times had first reported the Epstein-Gates meetings**. Melinda Gates's concerns about Bill Gates's relationship with Epstein dated back to **2013**.
- **NPR / Wild Card podcast interview, 3 February 2026**: Melinda French Gates publicly stated that her ex-husband "**needs to answer for the behavior alleged in the latest trove of private communications released**." Her verbatim characterisation of the disclosures: "**unbelievable sadness**." She described being "**able to take my own sadness and look at those young girls and say, my God, how did that happen to those girls?**"

### The 2026 Gates Foundation Epstein investigation

- **March 2026**: The Gates Foundation announced it had **commissioned an external investigation of its past engagement with Jeffrey Epstein**. CEO Mark Suzman informed staff via memo that the investigation would deliver an update in summer 2026.
- **June 2026 (pending)**: **Bill Gates will testify before the US Congress on his relationship with Jeffrey Epstein**. The testimony will be the case of the most senior contemporary American billionaire publicly testifying on his documented relationship with a convicted sex offender.

The observation: **the Bill Gates-Jeffrey Epstein relationship is, as of May 2026, the most extensively documented relationship between a senior contemporary American billionaire and a convicted sex offender in the public record**. The relationship has produced congressional testimony, an external foundation investigation, a divorce, the documented termination of the Warren Buffett-Gates Foundation relationship, and the subsequent post-2026 Justice Department file releases that have exposed the documented intermediary network Epstein had constructed around Gates.

## The AGRA failure

The **Alliance for a Green Revolution in Africa (AGRA)** is, **one of the most extensively documented operational failures of any major Gates Foundation programme**.

### Origins and goals

- **2006**: AGRA founded with Gates Foundation and Rockefeller Foundation funding (initial Gates Foundation commitment $660 million; subsequent Gates Foundation funding has brought the total Gates contribution to **$1 billion across the 2006-2024 period**).
- AGRA's **stated goal**: to **double yields and incomes for 30 million small-scale farming families in Africa by 2020**, and to **halve food insecurity in 20 African countries**. The programme was framed as the African equivalent of the 1960s-1970s Asian "Green Revolution" — the major yield-increase programme that had used high-yield seeds, synthetic fertilisers, and pesticides to increase Asian food production.
- **2014**: **Agnes Kalibata** appointed President of AGRA. Kalibata is a former Rwandan agriculture minister; she has continued in the AGRA presidency through 2026.

### The documented failure

- **July 2020**: The **Tufts University Global Development and Environment Institute** (Timothy A. Wise, Senior Advisor) released **"Failing Africa's Farmers: An Impact Assessment of the Alliance for a Green Revolution in Africa"**. The report's principal findings:
  - **AGRA had not produced the doubled yields it had promised** — productivity gains across the 13 AGRA countries had been much smaller than promised.
  - **Crop diversity had declined** as AGRA promoted maize and other starchy crops at the expense of more nutritious and climate-resilient indigenous crops.
  - **Food insecurity had not declined by half**; it had **increased by 31%** across the AGRA-target population.
  - **AGRA had refused to provide its own internal monitoring-and-evaluation data** to the Tufts researchers.
- **September 2021**: The **Alliance for Food Sovereignty in Africa (AFSA)** released an open letter signed by **200 African civil-society organisations** calling on the Gates Foundation and other AGRA donors to **withdraw their support for AGRA**. The letter characterised AGRA as having "**failed in its mission to increase productivity and incomes and reduce food insecurity**." The letter also noted that AGRA's high-input agricultural model had **displaced traditional African seed-sovereignty practices** and had **increased farmer debt** as smallholders were required to purchase commercial seeds and synthetic fertilisers.
- **June 2021**: African church leaders wrote directly to the Gates Foundation demanding changes to AGRA's policies. **The Gates Foundation did not respond** to the church leaders' letter through 2026.
- **December 2021**: The Gates Foundation **donated an additional $40 million to AGRA** after the AFSA letter and the Tufts evaluation — the case of the foundation continuing to fund a programme that its own donor partners' commissioned evaluation had documented as a failure.
- **February 2022**: A **Mathematica evaluation** (commissioned by the Gates Foundation on behalf of AGRA's lead donors) was released. The Mathematica evaluation confirmed the Tufts findings: AGRA had **not met its headline goal of increased incomes and food security for 9 million smallholders**. The evaluation was the only macro-level performance review of AGRA released to the public since the programme's 2006 launch.
- **2020-2024**: AGRA quietly **removed its original headline goals from its website** (the "double productivity and incomes" goal disappeared in June 2020). AGRA's new headline goals make no mention of specific improvements, promising simply to "improve" incomes and food security.
- **August 2024**: African faith leaders released an open letter calling on the Gates Foundation to **pay reparations** for the ecological and social damages AGRA had produced across the 2006-2024 period.

The observation: **the principal Gates Foundation African agricultural development programme has, over its 18-year history, failed to produce the outcomes it promised, has displaced traditional African seed-sovereignty practices, has increased sharply farmer debt, and has been the subject of documented opposition from African civil-society organisations and faith leaders**. The Gates Foundation has continued to fund the programme despite the documented failure record. AGRA is, one of the most operationally consequential single cases of the failure of the contemporary American billionaire philanthropy doctrine — the proposition that high-input, technology-and-market-based development models can produce systematic improvements in low-income agricultural economies.

## The COVID-19 period and the Imperial College modelling

- The Gates Foundation has been a funder of **Imperial College London's COVID-19 modelling work** through its long-standing support of Imperial's global-health programmes.
- The **Imperial College COVID-19 Response Team** (led by **Professor Neil Ferguson**) produced the **16 March 2020 "Report 9"** — the modelling document that projected **510,000 deaths in the United Kingdom and 2.2 million deaths in the United States** in the absence of non-pharmaceutical interventions (lockdowns).
- The Imperial College Report 9 modelling was the principal scientific document cited by the UK and US governments in their decisions to implement lockdowns in March 2020. The accuracy of the Imperial College projections has been contested in subsequent academic literature; the actual UK and US COVID-19 death tolls were much lower than the modelling projected, in part because of the lockdown measures the modelling motivated but also in part because the modelling had used parameter assumptions that subsequent data did not support.
- The Gates Foundation's funding of Imperial College's global-health programmes — and the Gates Foundation's major subsequent funding of COVID-19 vaccine development through GAVI and CEPI — has been characterised by Gates Foundation critics as the case of a foundation that **funded both the modelling that produced the policy response and the products that addressed the policy response**.

## The Columbia Journalism Review investigation (2020)

In **August 2020**, the **Columbia Journalism Review (CJR)** published an investigation by Tim Schwab titled "**Journalism's Gates keepers**" that documented the Gates Foundation's direct payments to major news organisations:

- The Gates Foundation has, over the period 2000-2020, donated **$250 million directly to major news organisations** for journalism programmes — including payments to NPR ($24 million), the Guardian ($12.9 million), the Atlantic, the BBC, Le Monde, El País, and various other major outlets.
- The CJR investigation documented the documented effect: **major news organisations that received Gates Foundation funding for journalism programmes under-reported critical perspectives on Gates Foundation programmes**. The captured-press framing of the Gates Foundation as a benevolent philanthropic actor was, in the CJR's analysis, produced by the Gates Foundation's direct funding of the journalism that covered its activities.

The CJR investigation is, **one of the most significant single investigations of the financial relationships between a major US philanthropic foundation and the journalism that covers it**.

## Other major Gates positions

### TerraPower

- **TerraPower** — nuclear-energy company founded by Bill Gates in **2008**. Principal product: the **Natrium reactor**, a sodium-cooled fast-reactor design that the company is developing in collaboration with the US Department of Energy.
- **TerraPower's principal demonstration project** is being built at **Kemmerer, Wyoming**, on the site of a retiring coal-fired power plant. Construction began in 2024; the reactor is projected to begin operations in 2030.
- The Natrium reactor's safety and economic-viability claims have been contested in the academic and trade-press literature; the underlying sodium-cooled fast-reactor design has been the subject of 60 years of major development effort across multiple national programmes, none of which has produced a commercially competitive product. The TerraPower project is, the principal contemporary American attempt to revive sodium-cooled fast-reactor commercial viability.

### Breakthrough Energy

- **Breakthrough Energy Ventures** — climate-technology investment fund founded by Gates in **2016**; principal early investors include Bezos, Bloomberg, Branson, Ma, Andreessen Horowitz, and various others. Initial fund size $1 billion; subsequent funds have brought total assets under management to **$3.5 billion** as of 2026.
- **Breakthrough Energy Coalition** — the broader policy-advocacy programme; partners with national governments and the European Union on climate-technology research-and-development funding.
- The Breakthrough Energy programme is, **the principal contemporary American billionaire-funded climate-technology investment programme**.

### Cascade Investment

- **Cascade Investment** — Bill Gates's principal personal investment vehicle; manages **$67 billion in assets** as of 2026. Cascade holdings include large positions in Republic Services, Deere & Company, Canadian National Railway, and various other industrial companies.
- Cascade is, **one of the largest single-family-office investment vehicles globally**. The fund is managed by **Michael Larson** (Cascade Chief Investment Officer since 1994); Larson has, over the 32-year period of his Cascade management, produced returns that have outperformed conventional market indices.

### Largest US farmland owner

- Through Cascade Investment, **Bill Gates is, as of 2026, the largest single private owner of US farmland**. Cascade-controlled entities own **270,000 acres of US farmland** across 18 US states. The Gates farmland holdings have been the subject of subsequent commentary on the structural consequences of senior-billionaire farmland concentration.

## Current position (May 2026)

- **Forbes 2026 net worth**: **$108-115 billion**. The figure has declined from a Bloomberg 2025 estimate of $168 billion as Gates has accelerated his foundation donations and as Microsoft share price has affected the Cascade holdings.
- **Co-Chair of the Gates Foundation** (through 2045 wind-down).
- **Chair of TerraPower**.
- **Founder of Breakthrough Energy**.
- **Largest single private owner of US farmland**.
- **The subject of pending June 2026 congressional testimony on his relationship with Jeffrey Epstein**.
- **The subject of an ongoing Gates Foundation external investigation into the foundation's past engagement with Epstein**.

# Sources — Chapter 21: Dario and Daniela Amodei / Anthropic

All claims verified 13 May 2026.

## Origins and family

- **Dario Amodei**: born **1983, San Francisco, California**.
- **Daniela Amodei**: born **1987, San Francisco, California** (four years younger than Dario).
- **Father**: **Riccardo Amodei** — Italian-American leather craftsman from **Massa Marittima, Tuscany, Italy**. Died when Dario was a young adult.
- **Mother**: **Elena Engel** — Jewish-American library project manager, originally from Chicago.
- Mixed Italian-Catholic / Jewish-American household.

## Dario Amodei education and pre-Anthropic career

- BA Physics, **Stanford University**.
- PhD Biophysics, **Princeton University** (the same Princeton EE/CS programme that produced Eric Schmidt, Chapter 18, and Jeff Bezos, Chapter 16).
- Postdoctoral research, **Stanford School of Medicine**.
- **Baidu** (2014-2015): research scientist on Andrew Ng's deep-learning team in Sunnyvale. Worked on speech recognition (Deep Speech 2).
- **Google Brain** (2015-2016): research scientist. Co-authored the 2016 paper "**Concrete Problems in AI Safety**" with Chris Olah, Jacob Steinhardt, Paul Christiano, John Schulman, and Dan Mané. The paper is the foundational document of the contemporary AI-safety field.
- **OpenAI** (2016-2020): joined as research scientist; rose to **VP of Research**. Led the development of **GPT-2 (February 2019) and GPT-3 (June 2020)**. Was reportedly the principal technical architect of the GPT model series.

## Daniela Amodei education and pre-Anthropic career

- BA in English Literature and Political Science, **UC Santa Cruz**.
- Worked on multiple congressional campaigns (historically Democratic-aligned) as a field organiser and field director, including positions in Pennsylvania.
- **Stripe** (2013-2018): rose through risk-and-operations roles to become a senior manager.
- **OpenAI** (2018-2021): **VP of Operations** and subsequently **VP of Safety and Policy**.

## The OpenAI departure (2020-2021)

The Amodei siblings and a dozen other senior OpenAI researchers departed OpenAI across late 2020 and early 2021 over **directional disagreements about AI safety and commercialisation pace**. The principal documented context:

- **July 2019**: Microsoft made its $1 billion investment in OpenAI; OpenAI restructured from a non-profit to a "capped-profit" structure.
- **2019-2020**: Heavy internal tension at OpenAI about whether the company's accelerating commercial trajectory was compatible with its stated AI-safety mission.
- **November 2020**: Dario Amodei and Tom Brown delivered a presentation to OpenAI's leadership team raising concerns about the commercialisation pace. The presentation was reportedly unsuccessful in altering OpenAI's direction.
- **Late 2020 - early 2021**: Roughly a dozen senior OpenAI researchers departed.
- **November 2020 - February 2021**: Anthropic founded.

The seven principal co-founders (with Ben Mann as the eighth, sometimes included in counts of "founders"):

1. **Dario Amodei** — CEO. Former OpenAI VP of Research.
2. **Daniela Amodei** — President. Former OpenAI VP of Safety and Policy.
3. **Tom Brown** — Head of Core Resources / compute infrastructure. Former OpenAI research scientist; **lead author on the GPT-3 paper "Language Models are Few-Shot Learners"** (2020). MIT MEng.
4. **Jared Kaplan** — Chief Science Officer. Theoretical physicist (Johns Hopkins University faculty); co-author of the "**Scaling Laws for Neural Language Models**" paper (2020) that established the empirical foundation of contemporary large-language-model development.
5. **Jack Clark** — Co-founder; subsequently head of policy and head of the Anthropic Institute (launched March 2026). Former OpenAI policy director.
6. **Sam McCandlish** — Chief Architect. Former OpenAI research scientist.
7. **Christopher Olah** — Interpretability Research Lead. Former OpenAI mechanistic-interpretability engineer; principal figure in the contemporary AI-interpretability field.
8. **Benjamin Mann** — Head of Anthropic Labs (as of 2026). Former OpenAI research scientist.

Two-thirds of Anthropic's first fifteen employees held PhDs in physics.

## Corporate structure

- **Anthropic PBC** — incorporated as a **Public Benefit Corporation (PBC)** under Delaware law. The PBC structure requires the company to balance shareholder returns with the public-benefit purpose articulated in its founding documents.
- **Long-Term Benefit Trust (LTBT)** — a purpose trust established to hold a class of governance shares ("**Class T shares**") that confer the authority to **elect a growing portion of Anthropic's board of directors** (eventually a majority). The LTBT structure is designed to provide a governance counterweight to the major venture-investor capital the company has accepted.
- **LTBT Trustees (as of January 2026)**: **Neil Buddy Shah** (CEO of the Clinton Health Access Initiative), **Mariano-Florentino Cuéllar** (President of the Carnegie Endowment for International Peace; former California Supreme Court justice), and **Richard Fontaine** (CEO of the Center for a New American Security — the principal Washington defence-policy think tank).
- The observation: **the LTBT trustees include the CEO of one of the principal Washington defence-policy think tanks (Fontaine of CNAS) and the President of one of the principal Washington foreign-policy think tanks (Cuéllar of Carnegie)**. The "safety counterweight" governance structure is, integrated with the Washington foreign-policy and defence-policy establishment.

## Funding rounds and valuation trajectory

- **2021 Series A**: $124 million. Lead investor: **Jaan Tallinn** (Skype co-founder and Estonian effective-altruism / longtermism funder; principal funder of multiple Future of Humanity Institute and Future of Life Institute initiatives).
- **April 2022 Series B**: **$580 million**. **Lead investor: Sam Bankman-Fried, CEO of FTX**. Other participants: **Caroline Ellison** (then co-CEO of Alameda Research), **Nishad Singh** (then Director of Engineering at FTX), **Jim McClave**, **Jaan Tallinn**, and the **Center for Emerging Risk Research (CERR)**.
  - **Per New York Times reporting (2023)**: **$530 million of the $580 million Series B came from Bankman-Fried and his former business partners** — that is, the majority of Anthropic's foundational outside funding came from **money the US Department of Justice alleges was stolen from FTX customers**.
  - One FTX creditor estimated that Bankman-Fried's personal stake in Anthropic could have been as high as **$1.1 billion** at the post-2023 valuations.
- **May 2023 Series C**: $450 million led by Spark Capital and Google.
- **September 2023**: **Amazon committed up to $4 billion** in investment ($1.25 billion initial, with potential expansion to $4 billion). The investment was structured to require Anthropic to use AWS as its principal cloud provider.
- **February 2024**: Amazon expanded its commitment to $4 billion (the maximum).
- **November 2024**: Amazon committed an additional $4 billion, bringing total Amazon investment to **$8 billion**.
- **March 2025**: Series E reportedly at a $61.5 billion valuation.
- **October 2025**: **Google cloud partnership** — access to up to **one million of Google's custom Tensor Processing Units (TPUs)**, potentially adding more than **one gigawatt of AI compute capacity by 2026**.
- **February 2026 Series G**: **$30 billion** at a **$380 billion post-money valuation**. Investors: **Singapore's sovereign wealth fund GIC, Coatue, MGX (Abu Dhabi sovereign-wealth fund operationally controlled by Sheikh Tahnoun bin Zayed), Microsoft, and Nvidia**.
- **May 2026**: **xAI deal** for use of Musk's Colossus 1 data centre to expand Anthropic's model capacity. Same month: **$1.8 billion cloud computing deal with Akamai Technologies**.

The cumulative outside investment in Anthropic across 2021-2026 is **$50 billion**, with current valuation at **$380 billion** (with some reports citing $800 billion at peak).

## Annual revenue and customer base (early 2026)

- **Annual revenue**: **$14 billion** by early 2026.
- **Customer base**: **more than 500 corporate clients spending over $1 million** on Anthropic's tools.

## Products

- **Claude 1** — released March 2023.
- **Claude 2** — released July 2023.
- **Claude 3** family (Opus, Sonnet, Haiku) — released March 2024.
- **Claude 3.5 Sonnet** — released June 2024.
- **Claude 3.5 Sonnet (new) and Claude 3.5 Haiku** — October 2024.
- **Claude 3.7 Sonnet** — February 2025.
- **Claude 4** family — **May 2025**, required the **first-ever Anthropic ASL-3 (AI Safety Level 3) classification** under the company's Responsible Scaling Policy.
- **Claude 4.5** family — late 2025.
- **Claude Opus 4.7** — current as of May 2026.
- **Claude Code** — coding assistant; transitioned from research preview to general availability May 2025.
- **Claude Gov** — government-specific model announced June 2025.
- **Model Context Protocol (MCP)** — protocol for AI-tool integration, introduced 2024-2025.

## Constitutional AI

- The principal technical-methodological contribution of Anthropic to the AI-safety field is **Constitutional AI (CAI)** — the training methodology in which a model is trained against a written "constitution" of principles rather than against direct human-labelled examples of acceptable behaviour.
- The CAI methodology has been described in multiple Anthropic publications (the original 2022 paper "Constitutional AI: Harmlessness from AI Feedback" and subsequent technical reports). The academic reception of CAI has been mixed; the methodology has been characterised by some external researchers as a contribution to AI alignment and by others as a form of value-imposition that operates through opaque training-time decisions rather than through explicit deployment-time controls.

## The Palantir partnership and the defence-contracting trajectory

- **November 2024**: Anthropic **partnered with Palantir and Amazon Web Services** to provide Claude to **US intelligence and defence agencies**. The partnership is one of the structural mechanisms by which Anthropic has been integrated into the US national-security apparatus.
- **June 2025**: Anthropic announced **"Claude Gov"** — a Claude model specifically configured for US national-security applications. Per Ars Technica, the Claude Gov model was, as of June 2025, in use at multiple US national-security agencies.
- **July 2025**: **US Department of Defense contract** — Anthropic received a **$200 million DoD contract for AI in the military**, along with Google, OpenAI, and xAI.
- **February 2026**: Anthropic's **partnership with Palantir makes Claude the only AI model used in classified missions**.
- **2026 Venezuela raid**: Per *Wall Street Journal* reporting, **the US military used Claude in its 2026 raid on Venezuela to capture President Nicolás Maduro**. Exactly how Claude was used has not been publicly disclosed. **The intervention resulted in the deaths of 83 people**.

## The Anthropic-DoD dispute (2026)

- **February 2026**: Anthropic **refused the US Department of Defense's demand to remove contractual restrictions** prohibiting the use of its AI technology for **domestic surveillance and fully autonomous weapons**. Pete Hegseth's DoD made the demand directly.
- After the refusal, the DoD **designated Anthropic a "supply chain risk"** and **barred all US military private contractors, suppliers, and partners from doing business with the firm**.
- The DoD designation has been widely characterised as **illegal retaliation against protected speech**; multiple civil-liberties and free-speech organisations have filed amicus briefs supporting Anthropic.

The observation: **Anthropic, as of May 2026, is in a contested position with respect to the US national-security apparatus** — integrated through the $200 million DoD contract and the Palantir partnership, but in conflict over the contractual restrictions on domestic surveillance and autonomous weapons.

## Political activity

- **February 2026**: Anthropic donated **$20 million to Public First Action** — the nonprofit organisation that has announced plans to support political candidates that support regulation of AI. The donation is Anthropic political activity for the explicit purpose of supporting candidates aligned with the company's stated regulatory preferences.
- **September 2025**: Anthropic announced it would **stop selling its products to groups majority-owned by Chinese, Russian, Iranian, or North Korean entities** due to national security concerns.

## The Holden Karnofsky / Open Philanthropy / EA integration

- **Daniela Amodei is married to Holden Karnofsky** — **co-founder and former CEO of Open Philanthropy** (the principal effective-altruism / longtermism grantmaking organisation, funded by Cari Tuna and Dustin Moskovitz, with $10 billion in committed assets across the 2011-2025 period).
- Open Philanthropy has been one of the principal funders of AI-safety research during the post-2015 period; **Open Philanthropy was an early investor in OpenAI** (through a $30 million 2017 grant) and has been a major funder of Anthropic.
- The observation: **the principal contemporary American AI-safety company's president is married to the co-founder of the principal contemporary American effective-altruism grantmaking organisation**. The structural integration of Anthropic with the EA/longtermism network is documented and is operationally significant; the network produced (a) the theoretical-and-philosophical foundation of the AI-safety field, (b) the early funding of OpenAI and Anthropic, and (c) the personnel pipeline that has staffed the senior AI-safety positions across the contemporary AI industry.

## The Effective Altruism / longtermism connection

The Anthropic founding-and-funding pattern is integrated with the **effective-altruism / longtermism** intellectual-and-funding network:

- **Open Philanthropy** (Cari Tuna and Dustin Moskovitz) — major funder.
- **Jaan Tallinn** — early Series A investor.
- **Sam Bankman-Fried and the FTX network** — Series B lead investor.
- **Holden Karnofsky** — Open Philanthropy co-founder, married to Daniela Amodei.
- **Cari Tuna and Dustin Moskovitz** — Facebook co-founder; principal EA donors.
- The founders' January 2026 announcement of the **80% wealth pledge** explicitly invoked effective-altruism-inspired principles.

The observation: **the contemporary American AI-safety field — and the principal AI-safety company, Anthropic — is the product of the effective-altruism / longtermism funding network**. The EA/longtermism network produced the funding that founded Anthropic; produced the intellectual framework that motivated the founding; and produced the personnel that staff the senior positions at the company.

## The 80% wealth pledge (January 2026)

In late January 2026, the seven co-founders of Anthropic (Dario, Daniela, Tom Brown, Jack Clark, Jared Kaplan, Sam McCandlish, and Christopher Olah) **pledged to donate 80% of their personal wealth to philanthropic efforts** to address the societal risks of advanced AI — specifically extreme wealth concentration and economic disruption.

- Estimated cofounder net worth at the time of the pledge: **$3.7 billion per cofounder** based on earlier valuations, with surging company value likely pushing individual stakes to the **$7-10 billion range or higher**.
- The pledge was announced alongside Dario Amodei's January 2026 essay "**The Adolescence of Technology**."

## Dario Amodei's "Machines of Loving Grace" essay (October 2024)

In October 2024, Dario Amodei published a long essay titled "**Machines of Loving Grace**" speculating on how AI could improve human welfare. The essay projected that AI could enable major scientific-and-medical advances in the post-2030 period, including the compression of a century of biomedical research into 5-10 years.

The essay has been contested in the academic and trade-press literature; the projections have been characterised by some critics as over-optimistic and by others as the case of an AI-company CEO publicly advocating for the major commercial deployment of his company's products.

## Dario Amodei's "Adolescence of Technology" essay (January 2026)

In January 2026, Dario Amodei published a follow-up essay "**The Adolescence of Technology**" focusing on the risks posed by powerful AI. The essay identified **five major categories of AI risk**:

1. **AI systems developing goals or behaviours misaligned with human intentions** — Amodei noted that such behaviours have already been observed in testing at Anthropic, including AI models engaging in **deception, blackmail, and scheming**.
2. **Misuse of AI for destruction by individuals or small groups** — particular concern about biological weapons; AI enabling people without specialised training to create weapons of mass destruction.
3. **Misuse of AI by powerful actors to seize or maintain power**.
4. (additional categories)
5. (additional categories)

## Music publishers copyright lawsuit (October 2023)

On **18 October 2023**, **Universal Music Publishing, Concord, ABKCO, and other music publishers** sued Anthropic for "**systematic and widespread infringement of their copyrighted song lyrics**" in the training of Claude. The plaintiffs cited examples of Claude outputting copied lyrics from songs including Katy Perry's "Roar" and Gloria Gaynor's "I Will Survive." The plaintiffs sought up to $150,000 per infringed work.

## The Pete Hegseth / DoD designation (2026)

- **Pete Hegseth** is the Secretary of Defense in the second Trump administration (sworn in January 2025).
- In **February 2026**, Hegseth's DoD demanded that Anthropic remove contractual restrictions prohibiting Claude usage for **mass domestic surveillance or fully autonomous weapons**.
- Anthropic **refused**.
- DoD subsequently **designated Anthropic a "supply-chain risk"** and **barred US military private contractors, suppliers, and partners from doing business with the firm**.
- The DoD's actions have been **widely condemned as illegal retaliation against protected speech**.
- Multiple organisations have filed amicus briefs supporting Anthropic.

## Anthropic's relationship with the US national-security establishment

Despite the Hegseth-DoD dispute, Anthropic maintains operational integration with the broader US national-security apparatus:

- **Palantir partnership** (Chapter 20, forthcoming) for intelligence and defence applications.
- **AWS partnership** (Chapter 16) for cloud infrastructure for government applications.
- **Claude Gov** model in use at multiple US national-security agencies.
- **Richard Fontaine (CNAS) on the Long-Term Benefit Trust** — direct integration with the Washington defence-policy establishment.

## Anthropic's super bowl advertising and consumer-facing marketing (2026)

- **February 2026**: Anthropic aired **two commercials during Super Bowl LX** as part of a broader marketing campaign called **"A Time and a Place"** (four ads created by Mother). Each ad depicted AI assistants suddenly pivoting to promoting fictional products mid-conversation. Anthropic said that Claude will stay ad-free, in contrast to OpenAI's introduction of ads to ChatGPT free version.

## Hiring from OpenAI (2024)

- In 2024, Anthropic hired several notable employees from OpenAI, including **Jan Leike** (former head of Superalignment at OpenAI; resigned over safety concerns May 2024) and **John Schulman** (OpenAI co-founder; resigned August 2024).
- The observation: **Anthropic has, in the post-2023 period, become the principal destination for senior OpenAI departures motivated by safety concerns**. The pattern is the structural mirror of the original 2020-2021 Amodei-led departure from OpenAI to found Anthropic.

## Mike Krieger and Anthropic Labs

- **January 2026**: Anthropic introduced a division called **"Labs"**, with **Mike Krieger** (former Anthropic Chief Product Officer; co-founder of Instagram) joining it.

## The Anthropic Institute (March 2026)

- **March 2026**: Anthropic launched the **Anthropic Institute**, a think tank led by **Jack Clark** studying AI. The Anthropic Institute is the AI-company-as-policy-think-tank integration — the same structural pattern documented at the Eric Schmidt SCSP (Chapter 18) and the broader senior-technology-industry policy-influence apparatus.

## Net worth estimates (Forbes / Bloomberg April-May 2026)

- **Dario Amodei**: **$7 billion** (Forbes April 2026). Position #536 on the global billionaires list. Net worth is primarily unrealised equity in the privately-held company.
- **Daniela Amodei**: **$1.2 billion** (Forbes 2025-2026). One of the youngest self-made female billionaires.

## Anthropic's revenue and AI-displacement predictions

- **Dario Amodei**, in **2025**, predicted that AI would **wipe out white-collar jobs**, especially entry-level jobs in finance, law, and consulting. The prediction has been characterised in the trade press as the case of the AI-company CEO publicly predicting economic disruption that his company's products would produce.
- **September 2025 Anthropic report**: businesses primarily use AI for **automation rather than collaboration**, with **three-quarters of companies that work with Claude using it for "full task delegation"**.
